#### #454. 4Sum II

[leetcode.com/problems/4sum-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/4sum-ii/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count quadruplets (i,j,k,l) such that A[i]+B[j]+C[k]+D[l]==0.

**Example:** `nums1 = [1,2], nums2 = [-2,-1], nums3 = [-1,2], nums4 = [0,2]` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int fourSumCount(int[] A, int[] B, int[] C, int[] D) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> countAB = new HashMap<>();
        for (int u : A) {
            for (int v : B) {
                countAB.put(u + v, countAB.getOrDefault(u + v, 0) + 1);
            }
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int u : C) {
            for (int v : D) {
                if (countAB.containsKey(-u - v)) {
                    result += countAB.get(-u - v);
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #457. Circular Array Loop

[leetcode.com/problems/circular-array-loop/](https://leetcode.com/problems/circular-array-loop/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Example:** `nums = [2,-1,1,2,2]` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Init slow=0, fast=0.
2. Advance fast through array; move slow only when condition met.
3. Write valid value at slow pointer position.
4. Return slow (length of valid prefix).
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean circularArrayLoop(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (nums[i] == 0) {
                continue;
            }
            int slow = i, fast = next(nums, i);
            //
            while (nums[slow] * nums[fast] > 0 && nums[slow] * nums[next(nums, fast)] > 0) {
                if (slow == fast) {
                    if (slow != next(nums, slow)) {
                        return true;
                    } else {
                        break;
                    }
                }
                slow = next(nums, slow);
                fast = next(nums, next(nums, fast));
            }
            int add = i;
            while (nums[add] * nums[next(nums, add)] > 0) {
                int temp = add;
                add = next(nums, add);
                nums[temp] = 0;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    public int next(int[] nums, int current) {
        int n = nums.length;
        return ((current + nums[current]) % n + n) % n; //  [0,n)
    }
}
class Solution {
    public boolean circularArrayLoop(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        //  vis
        //  i  idx  vis[i] = idx
        int[] vis = new int[n];
        for (int start = 0, index = 1; start < n; start++, index++) {
            if (vis[start] != 0) continue;
            int current = start;
            boolean flag = nums[current] > 0;
            while (true) {
                // nextfloor(next / n) * n
                // next
                int next = ((current + nums[current]) % n + n) % n;
                if (next == current) break;
                if (vis[next] != 0) {
                    //  next
                    if (vis[next] != index) break;
                    //  next
                    else return true;
                }
                if (flag && nums[next] < 0) break;
                if (!flag && nums[next] > 0) break;
                vis[next] = index;
                current = next;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

---

#### #485. Max Consecutive Ones

[leetcode.com/problems/max-consecutive-ones/](https://leetcode.com/problems/max-consecutive-ones/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the maximum number of consecutive 1s in a binary array.

**Example:** `nums = [1,1,0,1,1,1]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Init left=0, windowSum=0, result.
2. Expand right: add nums[right] to window.
3. If window exceeds size k: remove nums[left++].
4. Update result when window reaches target size.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findMaxConsecutiveOnes(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int left = 0, leftZeros = 0, rightZeros = 0;
        int result = 0;
        for(int right = 0; right < n; right++) {
            rightZeros += 1 - nums[right];
            while(rightZeros - leftZeros > 0) {
                leftZeros += 1 - nums[left];
                left++;
            }
            result = Math.max(result, right - left + 1);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

---

#### #493. Reverse Pairs

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-pairs/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-pairs/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Example:** `nums = [5,2,6,1]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int reversePairs(int[] nums) {
        if (nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        return reversePairsRecursive(nums, 0, nums.length - 1);
    }
    public int reversePairsRecursive(int[] nums, int left, int right) {
        if (left == right) {
            return 0;
        } else {
            int mid = (left + right) / 2;
            int n1 = reversePairsRecursive(nums, left, mid);
            int n2 = reversePairsRecursive(nums, mid + 1, right);
            int ret = n1 + n2;
            //
            int i = left;
            int j = mid + 1;
            while (i <= mid) {
                while (j <= right && (long) nums[i] > 2 * (long) nums[j]) {
                    j++;
                }
                ret += j - mid - 1;
                i++;
            }
            //
            int[] sorted = new int[right - left + 1];
            int p1 = left, p2 = mid + 1;
            int p = 0;
            while (p1 <= mid || p2 <= right) {
                if (p1 > mid) {
                    sorted[p++] = nums[p2++];
                } else if (p2 > right) {
                    sorted[p++] = nums[p1++];
                } else {
                    if (nums[p1] < nums[p2]) {
                        sorted[p++] = nums[p1++];
                    } else {
                        sorted[p++] = nums[p2++];
                    }
                }
            }
            for (int k = 0; k < sorted.length; k++) {
                nums[left + k] = sorted[k];
            }
            return ret;
        }
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

---

#### #498. Diagonal Traverse

[leetcode.com/problems/diagonal-traverse/](https://leetcode.com/problems/diagonal-traverse/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Traverse an m×n matrix diagonally, alternating direction.

**Example:** `mat = [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]]` → `[1,2,4,7,5,3,6,8,9]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] findDiagonalOrder(int[][] mat) {
        if(mat == null || mat.length == 0 || mat[0].length == 0) {
            return new int[0];
        }
        int m = mat.length, n = mat[0].length;
        int[] result = new int[m * n];
        int row = 0, col = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < m * n; i++) {
            result[i] = mat[row][col];
            if((row + col) % 2 == 0) {
                if(col == n - 1) {
                    row++;
                } else if(row == 0) {
                    col++;
                } else {
                    row--;
                    col++;
                }
            } else {
                if(row == m - 1) {
                    col++;
                } else if(col == 0) {
                    row++;
                } else {
                    row++;
                    col--;
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
here
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1424. Diagonal Traverse II

[leetcode.com/problems/diagonal-traverse-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/diagonal-traverse-ii/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Traverse a matrix diagonally from bottom-left to top-right.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] findDiagonalOrder(List<List<Integer>> nums) {
        int len = 0;
        Map<Integer,List<Integer>> map = new TreeMap<>();
        for(int i = 0;i < nums.size();i++) {
            len += nums.get(i).size(); //
            for(int j = 0;j < nums.get(i).size();j++) {
                map.computeIfAbsent(i + j, list -> new ArrayList<>()).add(nums.get(i).get(j));
            }
        }
        int[] result = new int[len];
        int index = 0;
        for(int key : map.keySet()) { // map
            List<Integer> list = map.get(key);
            for(int j = list.size() - 1;j >= 0;j--) { //
                result[index] = list.get(j);
                index++;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #517. Super Washing Machines

[leetcode.com/problems/super-washing-machines/](https://leetcode.com/problems/super-washing-machines/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findMinMoves(int[] machines) {
        int tot = Arrays.stream(machines).sum();
        int n = machines.length;
        if (tot % n != 0) {
            return -1;
        }
        int avg = tot / n;
        int result = 0, sum = 0;
        for (int num : machines) {
            num -= avg;
            sum += num;
            // A  B
            //
            result = Math.max(result, Math.max(Math.abs(sum), num));
        }
        return result;
    }
}
LCP 36.
class Solution {
    public int maxGroupNumber(int[] tiles) {
        int num,tol;
        Map<Integer, Integer> countMap = new HashMap<>();
        for (int tile : tiles) {
            countMap.put(tile,countMap.getOrDefault(tile,0)+1);
        }
        int[][] dp = new int[5][5];
        int[] array = Arrays.stream(tiles).distinct().sorted().toArray();
        for (int tile : array) {
            int[][] temp = new int[5][5];
            Integer current = countMap.getOrDefault(tile, 0);
            Integer pre = countMap.getOrDefault(tile-1, 0);
            Integer first = countMap.getOrDefault(tile-2, 0);
            for (int row = 0; row < Math.min(5,pre+1); row++) {
                for (int col = 0; col < Math.min(5,current+1); col++) {
                    num = Integer.MIN_VALUE;
                    tol = current - col;
                    for (int r = 0; r < Math.min(5,first+1); r++) {
                        for (int c = 0; c < Math.min(5-row,pre+1-row); c++) {
                            int shunzi = Math.min(Math.min(2,tol),Math.min(r,c));
                            for (int i = 0; i <= shunzi; i++) {
                                num = Math.max(num,i+(r-i)/3+(c-i)/3+(tol-i)/3+dp[r][c+row]);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    temp[row][col] = num;
                }
            }
            dp = temp;
        }
        return dp[0][0];
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #525. Contiguous Array

[leetcode.com/problems/contiguous-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/contiguous-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the maximum length subarray with equal number of 0s and 1s.

**Example:** `nums = [0,1]` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build prefix sum array for O(1) range queries.
2. prefix[i] = prefix[i-1] + nums[i-1].
3. Range sum [l,r] = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l].
4. Use HashMap to find complement prefix sums in O(1).
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    // 0  1 1  0  0 0  -1 0
    public int findMaxLength(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        Map<Integer, Integer> prefixMap = new HashMap<>();
        prefixMap.put(0, -1);
        int prefixSum = 0;
        int maxLen = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            prefixSum += (nums[i] == 0 ? -1 : 1);
            if(prefixMap.containsKey(prefixSum)) {
                maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, i - prefixMap.get(prefixSum));
            } else {
                prefixMap.put(prefixSum, i);
            }
        }
        return maxLen;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #532. K-diff Pairs in an Array

[leetcode.com/problems/kdiff-pairs-in-an-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/kdiff-pairs-in-an-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count unique k-diff pairs (i,j) in an array where |nums[i]-nums[j]|==k.

**Example:** `nums = [3,1,4,1,5], k = 2` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findPairs(int[] nums, int k) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        if(k == 0) {
            return findPair(nums);
        }
        int count = 0;
        Set<Integer> set = new HashSet<>();
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if(set.contains(nums[i])) {
                continue;
            }
            if(set.contains(k + nums[i])) {
                count++;
            }
            if(set.contains(-k + nums[i])) {
                count++;
            }
            set.add(nums[i]);
        }
        return count;
    }
    public int findPair(int[] nums) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        for(int num : nums) {
            map.put(num, map.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
        }
        int result = 0;
        for(int num : map.keySet()) {
            if(map.get(num) >= 2) {
                result++;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #566. Reshape the Matrix

[leetcode.com/problems/reshape-the-matrix/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reshape-the-matrix/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Reshape an m×n matrix to r×c without changing element order.

**Example:** `mat = [[1,2],[3,4]], r = 1, c = 4` → `[[1,2,3,4]]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[][] matrixReshape(int[][] nums, int r, int c) {
        int m = nums.length;
        int n = nums[0].length;
        if (m * n != r * c) {
            return nums;
        }
        int[][] result = new int[r][c];
        for (int x = 0; x < m * n; ++x) {
            // col
            result[x / c][x % c] = nums[x / n][x % n];
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #581. Shortest Unsorted Continuous Subarray

[leetcode.com/problems/shortest-unsorted-continuous-subarray/](https://leetcode.com/problems/shortest-unsorted-continuous-subarray/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the shortest subarray that, if sorted, makes the whole array sorted.

**Example:** `nums = [2,6,4,8,10,9,15]` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Init left=0, windowSum=0, result.
2. Expand right: add nums[right] to window.
3. If window exceeds size k: remove nums[left++].
4. Update result when window reaches target size.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findUnsortedSubarray(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int leftMax = Integer.MIN_VALUE, right = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (leftMax > nums[i]) {
                right = i;
            } else {
                leftMax = nums[i];
            }
        }
        int rightMin = Integer.MAX_VALUE, left = -1;
        for (int i = n-1; i >= 0; i--) {
            if (rightMin < nums[i]) {
                left = i;
            } else {
                rightMin = nums[i];
            }
        }
        return right == -1 ? 0 : right - left + 1;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #594. Longest Harmonious Subsequence

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-harmonious-subsequence/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-harmonious-subsequence/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Example:** `nums = [1,3,2,2,5,2,3,7]` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Init left=0, frequency map/counter, result.
2. Expand right: add s[right] to window state.
3. While window invalid: remove s[left] and advance left.
4. Update result with current window size/value.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    public int findLHS(int[] nums) {
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        int left = 0;
        int max = 0;
        for (int right = 0; right < nums.length; right++) {
            while (nums[right] - nums[left] > 1) {
                left++;
            }
            if (nums[right] - nums[left] == 1) {
                max = Math.max(max, right - left + 1);
            }
        }
        return max;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int findLHS(int[] nums) {
        HashMap <Integer, Integer> count = new HashMap <>();
        int result = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            count.put(num, count.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
        }
        for (int key : count.keySet()) {
            if (count.containsKey(key + 1)) {
                result = Math.max(result, count.get(key) + count.get(key + 1));
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #599. Minimum Index Sum of Two Lists

[leetcode.com/problems/minimum-index-sum-of-two-lists/](https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-index-sum-of-two-lists/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the common strings between two lists with the minimum index sum.

**Example:** `list1 = ["Shogun","Tapioca Express","Burger King"], list2 = ["Piatti","The Grill at Torrey Pines","Hungry Hunter Steakhouse","Shogun"]` → `["Shogun"]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
public class Solution {
    public String[] findRestaurant(String[] list1, String[] list2) {
        HashMap < String, Integer > map = new HashMap < String, Integer > ();
        for (int i = 0; i < list1.length; i++) {
            map.put(list1[i], i);
        }
        List < String > result = new ArrayList < > ();
        int min_sum = Integer.MAX_VALUE, sum;
        for (int j = 0; j < list2.length && j <= min_sum; j++) {
            if (map.containsKey(list2[j])) {
                sum = j + map.get(list2[j]);
                if (sum < min_sum) {
                    result.clear();
                    result.add(list2[j]);
                    min_sum = sum;
                } else if (sum == min_sum)
                result.add(list2[j]);
            }
        }
        return result.toArray(new String[result.size()]);
    }
}
Offer 57 - II. s
class Solution {
    public int[][] findContinuousSequence(int target) {
        List<int[]> vec = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int l = 1, r = 2; l < r;) {
            int sum = (l + r) * (r - l + 1) / 2;  [l, r]
            if (sum == target) {
                int[] result = new int[r - l + 1];
                for (int i = l; i <= r; ++i) {
                    result[i - l] = i;
                }
                vec.add(result);
                l++;
            } else if (sum < target) {
                r++;
            } else {
                l++;
            }
        }
        return vec.toArray(new int[vec.size()][]);
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int[][] findContinuousSequence(int target) {
        List<int[]> vec = new ArrayList<>();
        int sum = 0, limit = (target - 1) / 2; // (target - 1) / 2  target / 2
        for (int x = 1; x <= limit; ++x) {
            long delta = 1 - 4 * (x - (long) x * x - 2 * target);
            if (delta < 0) {
                continue;
            }
            int delta_sqrt = (int) Math.sqrt(delta + 0.5);
            if ((long) delta_sqrt * delta_sqrt == delta && (delta_sqrt - 1) % 2 == 0) {
                int y = (-1 + delta_sqrt) / 2; // (-1-delta_sqrt)/20
                if (x < y) {
                    int[] result = new int[y - x + 1];
                    for (int i = x; i <= y; ++i) {
                        result[i - x] = i;
                    }
                    vec.add(result);
                }
            }
        }
        return vec.toArray(new int[vec.size()][]);
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #611. Valid Triangle Number

[leetcode.com/problems/valid-triangle-number/](https://leetcode.com/problems/valid-triangle-number/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count the number of valid triangles from a sorted array of lengths.

**Example:** `nums = [2,2,3,4]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Init left=0, frequency map/counter, result.
2. Expand right: add s[right] to window state.
3. While window invalid: remove s[left] and advance left.
4. Update result with current window size/value.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    public int triangleNumber(int[] nums) {
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        int n = nums.length;
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 2; i--) {
            int left = 0, right = i - 1;
            while (left < right) {
                if (nums[left] + nums[right] > nums[i]) {
                    count += right - left;
                    right--;
                } else {
                    left++;
                }
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
class Solution {
    //
    public int triangleNumber(int[] nums) {
        radixSort(nums);
        int n = nums.length;
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 2; i--) {
            int left = 0, right = i - 1;
            while (left < right) {
                if (nums[left] + nums[right] > nums[i]) {
                    count += right - left;
                    right--;
                } else {
                    left++;
                }
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
    public void radixSort(int[] arr) {
        if(arr == null || arr.length < 2) {
            return ;
        }
        List<Integer>[] buckets = new List[10];
        for(int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
            buckets[i] = new ArrayList<>();
        }
        //
        for(int i = 0, mod = 10, div = 1; i < 10; i++, mod *= 10, div *= 10){
            //
            for(int j = 0; j < arr.length; j++){
                int num = (arr[j] % mod) / div;
                buckets[num].add(arr[j]);
            }
            //
            int index = 0;
            for(int j = 0; j < 10; j++) {
                for(int k = 0; k < buckets[j].size(); k++) {
                    arr[index++] = buckets[j].get(k);
                }
                buckets[j].clear();
            }
        }
    }
}
Offer 22. k LCOF
class Solution {
    public ListNode getKthFromEnd(ListNode head, int k) {
        if(head == null) {
            return head;
        }
        ListNode dummy = new ListNode(-1, head);
        ListNode fast = dummy.next;
        ListNode slow = dummy;
        for(int i = 0; i < k; i++) {
            fast = fast.next;
        }
        while(fast != null) {
            slow = slow.next;
            fast = fast.next;
        }
        return slow.next;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #661. Image Smoother

[leetcode.com/problems/image-smoother/](https://leetcode.com/problems/image-smoother/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[][] imageSmoother(int[][] img) {
        int rows = img.length, columns = img[0].length;
        int[][] result = new int[rows][columns];
        for(int i = 0; i < rows; i++) {
            for(int j = 0; j < columns; j++) {
                int count = 0;
                for (int nr = i-1; nr <= i+1; ++nr) {
                    for (int nc = j-1; nc <= j+1; ++nc) {
                        if (0 <= nr && nr < rows && 0 <= nc && nc < columns) {
                            result[i][j] += img[nr][nc];
                            count++;
                        }
                    }
                }
                result[i][j] /= count;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #723. Candy Crush

[leetcode.com/problems/candy-crush/](https://leetcode.com/problems/candy-crush/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Simulate Candy Crush: repeatedly remove groups of ≥3 same-color candies and drop.

**Example:** `board = [[R,R],[B,B]], pairs = [0,1]` → `[0,1]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[][] candyCrush(int[][] board) {
        int rows = board.length, cols = board[0].length;
        boolean todo = false;   //
        //
        for (int r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c + 2 < cols; c++) {
                //
                int v = Math.abs(board[r][c]);
                //
                if (v != 0 && v == Math.abs(board[r][c + 1]) && v == Math.abs(board[r][c + 2])) {
                    //
                    board[r][c] = board[r][c + 1] = board[r][c + 2] = -v;
                    todo = true;
                }
            }
        }
        //
        for (int r = 0; r + 2 < rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
                //
                int v = Math.abs(board[r][c]);
                //
                if (v != 0 && v == Math.abs(board[r + 1][c]) && v == Math.abs(board[r + 2][c])) {
                    //
                    board[r][c] = board[r + 1][c] = board[r + 2][c] = -v;
                    todo = true;
                }
            }
        }
        //  for
        //
        if (todo) {
            for (int c = 0; c < cols; c++) {   //
                int wr = rows - 1;  //
                for (int r = rows - 1; r >= 0; r--) {  //
                    if (board[r][c] > 0) {
                        //  (r,c)  (wr,c)
                        // r  wr  r  wr
                        board[wr][c] = board[r][c];
                        wr--;   //
                    }
                }
                //  wr
                while (wr >= 0) {
                    board[wr--][c] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
        //  candyCrush(board)
        //  candyCrush  candyCrush
        //  candyCrush  for  tod0  true
        //  candyCrush
        return todo ? candyCrush(board) : board;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #724. Find Pivot Index

[leetcode.com/problems/find-pivot-index/](https://leetcode.com/problems/find-pivot-index/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find a pivot index where left sum equals right sum.

**Example:** `nums = [1,7,3,6,5,6]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build prefix sum array for O(1) range queries.
2. prefix[i] = prefix[i-1] + nums[i-1].
3. Range sum [l,r] = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l].
4. Use HashMap to find complement prefix sums in O(1).
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int pivotIndex(int[] nums) {
        int total = Arrays.stream(nums).sum();
        int prefixSum = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if(2 * prefixSum + nums[i] == total) {
                return i;
            }
            prefixSum += nums[i];
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #766. Toeplitz Matrix

[leetcode.com/problems/toeplitz-matrix/](https://leetcode.com/problems/toeplitz-matrix/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Check if a matrix is Toeplitz (all diagonals have the same value).

**Example:** `matrix = [[1,2],[2,2]]` → `false`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isToeplitzMatrix(int[][] matrix) {
        int m = matrix.length, n = matrix[0].length;
        for (int i = 1; i < m; i++) {
            for (int j = 1; j < n; j++) {
                if (matrix[i][j] != matrix[i - 1][j - 1]) {
                    return false;
                }
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #769. Max Chunks To Make Sorted

[leetcode.com/problems/max-chunks-to-make-sorted/](https://leetcode.com/problems/max-chunks-to-make-sorted/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Example:** `arr = [4,3,2,1,0]` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    // ? ; 0  n  p  q  q + 1  p
    public int maxChunksToSorted(int[] arr) {
        int max = -1;
        int result = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
            max = Math.max(max, arr[i]);
            if(max == i) {
                result++;
                max = -1;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #795. Number of Subarrays with Bounded Maximum

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-subarrays-with-bounded-maximum/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-subarrays-with-bounded-maximum/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count subarrays with a maximum value in range [L, R].

**Example:** `nums = [2,1,4,3], left = 2, right = 3` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Count subarrays with max <= R minus subarrays with max <= L-1 (inclusion-exclusion). Single pass: track current run length where all elements <= bound.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int numSubarrayBoundedMax(int[] nums, int left, int right) {
        return count(nums, right) - count(nums, left - 1);
    }
    private int count(int[] nums, int bound) {
        int result = 0, current = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            current = num <= bound ? current + 1 : 0;  // extend or reset run
            result += current;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Alternative: single pass tracking dp = count of valid subarrays ending here.
- Handles negative numbers without changes.

#### #807. Max Increase to Keep City Skyline

[leetcode.com/problems/max-increase-to-keep-city-skyline/](https://leetcode.com/problems/max-increase-to-keep-city-skyline/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Maximize the total increase in building heights while preserving the city's skyline from all 4 directions.

**Example:** `grid = [[3,0,8,4],[2,4,5,7],[9,2,6,3],[0,3,1,0]]` → `35`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  grid  grid
    public int maxIncreaseKeepingSkyline(int[][] grid) {
        int n = grid.length;
        int[] rowMax = new int[n];
        int[] colMax = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
                rowMax[i] = Math.max(rowMax[i], grid[i][j]);
                colMax[j] = Math.max(colMax[j], grid[i][j]);
            }
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
                result += Math.min(rowMax[i], colMax[j]) - grid[i][j];
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #825. Friends Of Appropriate Ages

[leetcode.com/problems/friends-of-appropriate-ages/](https://leetcode.com/problems/friends-of-appropriate-ages/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count pairs of people where one person's age allows them to friend the other.

**Example:** `ages = [16,16]` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
//
// O(n)
class Solution {
    public int numFriendRequests(int[] ages) {
        if(ages == null || ages.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int[] buckets = new int[121];
        for(int age : ages) {
            buckets[age]++;
        }
        int count = 0;
        for(int x = 1; x <= 120; x++) {
            for(int y = 1; y <= 120; y++) {
                if(y > x || (y > 100 && x < 100) || y <= 0.5 * x + 7) {
                    continue;
                }
                if(x == y) {
                    count += buckets[x] * (buckets[x] - 1);
                    continue;
                }
                count += buckets[x] * buckets[y];
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #838. Push Dominoes

[leetcode.com/problems/push-dominoes/](https://leetcode.com/problems/push-dominoes/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Given a string of dominoes ('L', 'R', '.'), simulate their fall.

**Example:** `dominoes = "RR..LL"` → `"RRLLLL"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String pushDominoes(String S) {
        char[] A = S.toCharArray();
        int N = A.length;
        int[] forces = new int[N];
        // Populate forces going from left to right
        int force = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
            if (A[i] == 'R') force = N;
            else if (A[i] == 'L') force = 0;
            else force = Math.max(force - 1, 0);
            forces[i] += force;
        }
        // Populate forces going from right to left
        force = 0;
        for (int i = N-1; i >= 0; --i) {
            if (A[i] == 'L') force = N;
            else if (A[i] == 'R') force = 0;
            else force = Math.max(force - 1, 0);
            forces[i] -= force;
        }
        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        for (int f: forces)
        result.append(f > 0 ? 'R' : f < 0 ? 'L' : '.');
        return result.toString();
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #845. Longest Mountain in Array

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-mountain-in-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-mountain-in-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the length of the longest mountain subarray (increases then decreases).

**Example:** `nums = [2,1,4,7,3,2,5]` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int longestMountain(int[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;
        if (n == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int[] left = new int[n];
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
            left[i] = arr[i - 1] < arr[i] ? left[i - 1] + 1 : 0;
        }
        int[] right = new int[n];
        for (int i = n - 2; i >= 0; --i) {
            right[i] = arr[i + 1] < arr[i] ? right[i + 1] + 1 : 0;
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (left[i] > 0 && right[i] > 0) {
                result = Math.max(result, left[i] + right[i] + 1);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #896. Monotonic Array

[leetcode.com/problems/monotonic-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/monotonic-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Check if an array is monotonically increasing or decreasing.

**Example:** `nums = [1,2,2,3]` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isMonotonic(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        boolean monoIncrease = true;
        boolean monoDecrease = true;
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length - 1; i++) {
            if(nums[i] > nums[i+1]) {
                monoDecrease = false;
            }
            if(nums[i] < nums[i+1]) {
                monoIncrease = false;
            }
        }
        return monoDecrease || monoIncrease;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #912. Sort an Array

[leetcode.com/problems/sort-an-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/sort-an-array/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Example:** `nums = [5,2,3,1]` → `[1,2,3,5]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] sortArray(int[] nums) {
        quickSort(nums);
        mergeSort(nums);
        return nums;
    }
    public void mergeSort(int[] nums) {
        mergeSort(nums, 0, nums.length, new int[nums.length]);
    }
    private void mergeSort(int[] nums, int start, int end, int[] temp) {
        if(start == end || start + 1 == end) {
            return ;
        }
        int mid = start + (end - start) / 2;
        mergeSort(nums, start, mid, temp); // [start, mid)
        mergeSort(nums, mid, end, temp); // [mid, end)
        int i = start, j = mid, k = start;
        while(i < mid || j < end) {
            if(j >= end || i < mid && nums[i] < nums[j]) {
                temp[k++] = nums[i++];
            } else {
                temp[k++] = nums[j++];
            }
        }
        for(i = start; i < end; i++) {
            nums[i] = temp[i];
        }
    }
    public void quickSort(int[] nums) {
        quickSort(nums, 0, nums.length, new int[nums.length]);
    }
    private void quickSort(int[] nums, int start, int end, int[] temp) {
        // [start, end)
        if(start == end || start + 1 == end) {
            return ;
        }
        int mid = start + (end - start) / 2;
        int pivot = nums[mid];
        int left = start, current = start, right = end - 1;
        // [start, left)
        // [left, current)
        // [current, right]
        // [current, end)
        while(current <= right) {
            if(nums[current] < pivot) {
                swap(nums, current, left);
                current++;
                left++;
            } else if(nums[current] == pivot) {
                current++;
            } else {
                swap(nums, current, right);
                right--;
            }
        }
        quickSort(nums, start, left, temp);
        quickSort(nums, current, end, temp);
    }
    private void swap(int[] nums, int i, int j) {
        int temp = nums[i];
        nums[i] = nums[j];
        nums[j] = temp;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

---

#### #977. Squares of a Sorted Array

[leetcode.com/problems/squares-of-a-sorted-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/squares-of-a-sorted-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Return the squares of a sorted array in sorted order.

**Example:** `nums = [-4,-1,0,3,10]` → `[0,1,9,16,100]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] sortedSquares(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int[] result = new int[n];
        for (int left = 0, right = n - 1, pos = n - 1; left <= right;) {
            if (nums[left] * nums[left] > nums[right] * nums[right]) {
                result[pos] = nums[left] * nums[left];
                ++left;
            } else {
                result[pos] = nums[right] * nums[right];
                --right;
            }
            --pos;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1013. Partition Array Into Three Parts With Equal Sum

[leetcode.com/problems/partition-array-into-three-parts-with-equal-sum/](https://leetcode.com/problems/partition-array-into-three-parts-with-equal-sum/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canThreePartsEqualSum(int[] A) {
        int s = 0;
        for (int num : A) {
            s += num;
        }
        if (s % 3 != 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int target = s / 3;
        int n = A.length, i = 0, current = 0;
        while (i < n) {
            current += A[i];
            if (current == target) {
                break;
            }
            i++;
        }
        if (current != target) {
            return false;
        }
        int j = i + 1;
        while (j + 1 < n) {  //
            current += A[j];
            if (current == target * 2) {
                return true;
            }
            j++;
        }
        return false;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1074. Number of Submatrices That Sum to Target

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-submatrices-that-sum-to-target/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-submatrices-that-sum-to-target/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int numSubmatrixSumTarget(int[][] matrix, int target) {
        int result = 0;
        int m = matrix.length, n = matrix[0].length;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) { //
            int[] sum = new int[n];
            for (int j = i; j < m; ++j) { //
                for (int c = 0; c < n; ++c) {
                    sum[c] += matrix[j][c]; //
                }
                result += subarraySum(sum, target);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
    public int subarraySum(int[] nums, int k) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        map.put(0, 1);
        int count = 0, pre = 0;
        for (int x : nums) {
            pre += x;
            if (map.containsKey(pre - k)) {
                count += map.get(pre - k);
            }
            map.put(pre, map.getOrDefault(pre, 0) + 1);
        }
        return count;
    }
}
17.14. Smallest K LCCI
class Solution {
    public int[] smallestK(int[] arr, int k) {
        quickSelect(arr, k);
        int[] result = new int[k];
        for(int i = 0; i < k; i++) {
            result[i] = arr[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
    private void swap(int[] nums, int i, int j) {
        int temp = nums[i];
        nums[i] = nums[j];
        nums[j] = temp;
    }
    private void quickSelect(int[] nums, int k) {
        quickSelect(nums, 0, nums.length, k);
    }
    private void quickSelect(int[] nums, int start, int end, int k) {
        // [start, end)
        if(start == end || start + 1 == end) {
            return ;
        }
        int mid = start + (end - start) / 2;
        int pivot = nums[mid];
        int left = start, current = start, right = end - 1;
        // [start, left)
        // [left, current)
        // [current, right]
        // [current, end)
        while(current <= right) {
            if(nums[current] < pivot) {
                swap(nums, current, left);
                current++;
                left++;
            } else if(nums[current] == pivot) {
                current++;
            } else {
                swap(nums, current, right);
                right--;
            }
        }
        int leftCount = left - start;
        int midCount = current - left;
        int rightCount = end - current;
        if(k <= leftCount) {
            quickSelect(nums, start, left, k);
        } else if(k <= leftCount + midCount) {
            return ;
        } else {
            quickSelect(nums, right + 1, end, k - leftCount - midCount);
        }
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #1109. Corporate Flight Bookings

[leetcode.com/problems/corporate-flight-bookings/](https://leetcode.com/problems/corporate-flight-bookings/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
5:
treemap
class Solution {
    // booking=[l,r,inc]booking=[l,r,inc]
    // d[l]  incd[r+1]  inc
    public int[] corpFlightBookings(int[][] bookings, int n) {
        int[] arr = new int[n+2];
        for(int[] booking : bookings) {
            arr[booking[0]] += booking[2];
            arr[booking[1] + 1] -= booking[2];
        }
        int[] result = new int[n];
        int sum = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            sum += arr[i+1];
            result[i] = sum;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #1213. Intersection of Three Sorted Arrays

[leetcode.com/problems/intersection-of-three-sorted-arrays/](https://leetcode.com/problems/intersection-of-three-sorted-arrays/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the intersection of three sorted arrays.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<Integer> arraysIntersection(int[] arr1, int[] arr2, int[] arr3) {
        List<Integer> list = new ArrayList<>();
        int i = 0, j = 0, k = 0;
        int minValue = 0;
        while(i < arr1.length && j < arr2.length && k < arr3.length){
            if(arr1[i] == arr2[j] && arr2[j] == arr3[k]){
                list.add(arr1[i]);
                i++;
                j++;
                k++;
            }else{
                minValue = Math.min(Math.min(arr1[i], arr2[j]), arr3[k]);
                if(arr1[i] == minValue) i++;
                if(arr2[j] == minValue) j++;
                if(arr3[k] == minValue) k++;
            }
        }
        return list;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1275. Find Winner on a Tic Tac Toe Game

[leetcode.com/problems/find-winner-on-a-tic-tac-toe-game/](https://leetcode.com/problems/find-winner-on-a-tic-tac-toe-game/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Determine the winner of a Tic-Tac-Toe game given a sequence of moves.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  A  B
    public String tictactoe(int[][] moves) {
        int m = moves.length;
        // 0-20-23
        // count[0-2]0-2count[3-5]0-2count[6]count[7]
        int[] count = new int[8];
        // 2
        //
        for(int i = m - 1; i >= 0; i -= 2) {
            //
            count[moves[i][0]]++;
            //
            count[moves[i][1] + 3]++;
            //
            if(moves[i][0] == moves[i][1])
            count[6]++;
            //  (
            // 3x3
            if(moves[i][0] + moves[i][1] == 2)
            count[7]++;
            // 3
            if(count[moves[i][0]] == 3 || count[moves[i][1] + 3] == 3 ||
            count[6] == 3 || count[7] == 3)
            // AB  B A
            return m % 2 == 0 ? "B" : "A";
        }
        //
        if(moves.length < 9)
        return "Pending";
        //
        return "Draw";
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #1351. Count Negative Numbers in a Sorted Matrix

[leetcode.com/problems/count-negative-numbers-in-a-sorted-matrix/](https://leetcode.com/problems/count-negative-numbers-in-a-sorted-matrix/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count the number of negative numbers in a sorted matrix.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int countNegatives(int[][] grid) {
        int rows = grid.length, columns = grid[0].length;
        int i = rows - 1, j = 0;
        int count = 0;
        while(i >= 0 && j < columns) {
            if(grid[i][j] < 0) {
                count += columns - j;
                i--;
            } else {
                j++;
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #1460. Make Two Arrays Equal by Reversing Sub-arrays

[leetcode.com/problems/make-two-arrays-equal-by-reversing-subarrays/](https://leetcode.com/problems/make-two-arrays-equal-by-reversing-subarrays/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Check if two arrays become equal after reversing any sub-array of one.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canBeEqual(int[] target, int[] arr) {
        int[] bucket1 = new int[1001];
        int[] bucket2 = new int[1001];
        for(int i = 0; i < target.length; i++) {
            bucket1[target[i]]++;
            bucket2[arr[i]]++;
        }
        for(int i  = 0; i <= 1000; i++) {
            if(bucket1[i] != bucket2[i]) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1480. Running Sum of 1d Array

[leetcode.com/problems/running-sum-of-1d-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/running-sum-of-1d-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Return the running sum of an array.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] runningSum(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            nums[i] += nums[i - 1];
        }
        return nums;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

---

#### #1498. Number of Subsequences That Satisfy the Given Sum Condition

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-subsequences-that-satisfy-the-given-sum-condition/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-subsequences-that-satisfy-the-given-sum-condition/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count the number of subsequences where min+max ≤ target.

**Algorithm:**
1. Sort array. Two pointers: left=0, right=n-1. When nums[left]+nums[right]<=target, all 2^(right-left) subsets with this left endpoint are valid. Precompute powers of 2 mod 1e9+7.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int numSubseq(int[] nums, int target) {
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        int n = nums.length, MOD = 1_000_000_007;
        long[] pow = new long[n];
        pow[0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) pow[i] = pow[i - 1] * 2 % MOD;
        int result = 0, left = 0, right = n - 1;
        while (left <= right) {
            if (nums[left] + nums[right] <= target) {
                result = (int)((result + pow[right - left]) % MOD);  // 2^(r-l) subsequences
                left++;
            } else {
                right--;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Skip precomputing pow[] by computing inline with modular fast-pow.
- Monotonic: once nums[left]+nums[right] > target, all rights smaller than right also fail for current left.

---

#### #1748. Sum of Unique Elements

[leetcode.com/problems/sum-of-unique-elements/](https://leetcode.com/problems/sum-of-unique-elements/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the sum of all unique elements in an array.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int sumOfUnique(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        Set<Integer> unique = new HashSet<>();
        Set<Integer> others = new HashSet<>();
        for(int num : nums) {
            if(!others.contains(num) && !unique.contains(num)) {
                unique.add(num);
            } else if(unique.contains(num)) {
                unique.remove(num);
                others.add(num);
            }
        }
        int sum = 0;
        for(int num : unique) {
            sum += num;
        }
        return sum;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int sumOfUnique(int[] nums) {
        int[] num = new int[101];
        int result = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if(num[nums[i]] == 1) {
                result -= nums[i];
            }else if(num[nums[i]] == 0){
                result += nums[i];
            }
            num[nums[i]]++;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1762. Buildings With an Ocean View

[leetcode.com/problems/buildings-with-an-ocean-view/](https://leetcode.com/problems/buildings-with-an-ocean-view/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find all buildings with an ocean view (nothing taller to their right).

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] findBuildings(int[] heights) {
        if(heights == null || heights.length == 0) {
            return new int[]{};
        }
        int rightMax = 0;
        List<Integer> list = new ArrayList<>();
        for(int i = heights.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            if(heights[i] > rightMax) {
                list.add(i);
            }
            rightMax = Math.max(rightMax, heights[i]);
        }
        int[] result = new int[list.size()];
        for(int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++) {
            result[i] = list.get(list.size() - i - 1);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1868. Product of Two Run-Length Encoded Arrays

[leetcode.com/problems/product-of-two-runlength-encoded-arrays/](https://leetcode.com/problems/product-of-two-runlength-encoded-arrays/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Multiply two run-length encoded arrays.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<List<Integer>> findRLEArray(int[][] encoded1, int[][] encoded2) {
        List<List<Integer>> list = new ArrayList<>();
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while(i < encoded1.length && j < encoded2.length) {
            int count = Math.min(encoded1[i][1], encoded2[j][1]);
            int product = encoded1[i][0] * encoded2[j][0];
            if(list.size() > 0 && list.get(list.size() - 1).get(0) == product) {
                int previousCount = list.get(list.size() - 1).get(1);
                list.get(list.size() - 1).set(1, previousCount + count);
            } else {
                list.add(Arrays.asList(product, count));
            }
            encoded1[i][1] -= count;
            encoded2[j][1] -= count;
            if(encoded1[i][1] == 0) {
                i++;
            }
            if(encoded2[j][1] == 0) {
                j++;
            }
        }
        return list;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1877. Minimize Maximum Pair Sum in Array

[leetcode.com/problems/minimize-maximum-pair-sum-in-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/minimize-maximum-pair-sum-in-array/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Minimize the maximum pair sum after optimally pairing elements.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int minPairSum(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int max = 0;
        Arrays.sort(nums);
        for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; ++i) {
            max = Math.max(max, nums[i] + nums[n - 1 - i]);
        }
        return max;
    }
}
```

- After sorting, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of linear scan for lookups.

#### #1893. Check if All the Integers in a Range Are Covered

[leetcode.com/problems/check-if-all-the-integers-in-a-range-are-covered/](https://leetcode.com/problems/check-if-all-the-integers-in-a-range-are-covered/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Check if all integers in [left, right] are covered by at least one given range.

**Algorithm:**
1. Sort array to enable two-pointer convergence.
2. Set left=0, right=n-1.
3. If sum/condition met: record result, move both pointers.
4. If too small: advance left. If too large: retreat right.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isCovered(int[][] ranges, int left, int right) {
        int[] diff = new int[52];   //
        for (int[] range : ranges) {
            ++diff[range[0]];
            --diff[range[1] + 1];
        }
        //
        int current = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i <= 50; ++i) {
            current += diff[i];
            if (left <= i && i <= right && current <= 0) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public boolean isCovered(int[][] ranges, int left, int right) {
        Arrays.sort(ranges, (a1, a2) -> a1[0] - a2[0]);
        for(int[] range: ranges) {
            int l = range[0];
            int r = range[1];
            if(l <= left && left <= r) {
                left = r + 1;
            }
        }
        return left > right;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1894. Find the Student that Will Replace the Chalk

[leetcode.com/problems/find-the-student-that-will-replace-the-chalk/](https://leetcode.com/problems/find-the-student-that-will-replace-the-chalk/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the index of the student who will replace the chalk.

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int chalkReplacer(int[] chalk, int k) {
        int n = chalk.length;
        long total = 0;
        for (int num : chalk) {
            total += num;
        }
        //
        k %= total;
        int result = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (chalk[i] > k) {
                result = i;
                break;
            }
            k -= chalk[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    //  chalk
    public int chalkReplacer(int[] chalk, int k) {
        int n = chalk.length;
        if (chalk[0] > k) {
            return 0;
        }
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
            chalk[i] += chalk[i - 1];
            //  k
            if (chalk[i] > k) {
                return i;
            }
        }
        k %= chalk[n - 1];
        return binarySearch(chalk, k);
    }
    public int binarySearch(int[] arr, int target) {
        int low = 0, high = arr.length - 1;
        while (low < high) {
            int mid = (high - low) / 2 + low;
            if (arr[mid] <= target) {
                low = mid + 1;
            } else {
                high = mid;
            }
        }
        return low;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1920. Build Array from Permutation

[leetcode.com/problems/build-array-from-permutation/](https://leetcode.com/problems/build-array-from-permutation/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Build an array where result[i] = nums[nums[i]].

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  1000
    // 1000
    public int[] buildArray(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            nums[i] += 1000 * (nums[nums[i]] % 1000);
        }
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            nums[i] /= 1000;
        }
        return nums;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

#### #1929. Concatenation of Array

[leetcode.com/problems/concatenation-of-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/concatenation-of-array/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Return the concatenation of an array with itself.

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] getConcatenation(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int[] result = new int[n * 2];
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            result[i] = nums[i];
            result[i + n] = nums[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Sort + two pointers reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n log n).
- Use prefix sums to answer range queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.
