#### #413. Arithmetic Slices

[leetcode.com/problems/arithmetic-slices/](https://leetcode.com/problems/arithmetic-slices/) · `O(n²) / O(n)`

**Q:** Count the number of arithmetic slices (subarrays of length ≥3 with equal differences).

**Example:** `nums = [1,2,3,4]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n²) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int numberOfArithmeticSlices(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 2; ) {
            int j = i, d = nums[i + 1] - nums[i];
            while (j + 1 < n && nums[j + 1] - nums[j] == d) j++;
            int len = j - i + 1;
            // a1 len an 3
            int a1 = 1, an = len - 3 + 1;
            // 3
            int count = (a1 + an) * an / 2;
            result += count;
            i = j;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int numberOfArithmeticSlices(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int count = 0;
        int[] dp = new int[n];
        for(int i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            if(nums[i] - nums[i-1] == nums[i-1] - nums[i-2]) {
                dp[i] = dp[i-1] + 1;
                count += dp[i];
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int numberOfArithmeticSlices(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int count = 0;
        int[] dp = new int[n];
        int previous = 0;
        int current;
        for(int i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            current = 0;
            if(nums[i] - nums[i-1] == nums[i-1] - nums[i-2]) {
                current = previous + 1;
                count += current;
            }
            previous = current;
        }
        return count;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int numberOfArithmeticSlices(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int count = 0;
        int i = 0;
        while(i < n - 2) {
            int d = nums[i + 1] - nums[i];
            int len = 1;
            while(i + 1 < n && nums[i+1] - nums[i] == d) {
                i++;
                len++;
                if(len >= 3) {
                    count += (len - 3 + 1);
                }
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```

- Use two variables instead of array if only last 1-2 states needed — O(1) space.

---

#### #416. Partition Equal Subset Sum

[leetcode.com/problems/partition-equal-subset-sum/](https://leetcode.com/problems/partition-equal-subset-sum/) · `O(n * sum) / O(sum)`

**Q:** Determine if an integer array can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.

**Example:** `nums = [1,5,11,5]` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. If sum is odd, return false.
2. /1 Knapsack: dp[j] = can subset sum to j.
3. For each num: traverse dp backward from target to num; dp[j] |= dp[j-num].
`O(n * sum) / O(sum)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean canPartition(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        if (n < 2) {
            return false;
        }
        int sum = 0, maxNum = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            sum += num;
            maxNum = Math.max(maxNum, num);
        }
        if (sum % 2 != 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int target = sum / 2;
        if (maxNum > target) {
            return false;
        }
        boolean[][] dp = new boolean[n][target + 1];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            dp[i][0] = true;
        }
        dp[0][nums[0]] = true;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            int num = nums[i];
            for (int j = 1; j <= target; j++) {
                if (j >= num) {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i - 1][j] | dp[i - 1][j - num];
                } else {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i - 1][j];
                }
            }
        }
        return dp[n - 1][target];
    }
}
```

- Backward traversal of dp ensures each element is used at most once (0/1 knapsack).

---

#### #494. Target Sum

[leetcode.com/problems/target-sum/](https://leetcode.com/problems/target-sum/) · `O(n * sum) / O(sum)`

**Q:** Count the number of ways to assign + or - to array elements to reach a target sum.

**Example:** `nums = [1,1,1,1,1], target = 3` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Convert to subset sum: count subsets with sum = (target+total)/2.
2. /1 knapsack: dp[j] += dp[j-num] traversed backward.
`O(n * sum) / O(sum)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findTargetSumWays(int[] nums, int target) {
        // A - B = target;
        // A + B = sum
        // A = (sum + target) / 2;
        // B = (sum - target) / 2;
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int sum = 0;
        for(int num : nums) {
            sum += num;
        }
        int n = nums.length;
        if(sum - target < 0 || (sum - target) % 2 != 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        target = (sum - target) / 2;
        int[] dp = new int[target+1];
        dp[0] = 1;
        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            for(int j = target; j >= 0; j--) {
                if(j - nums[i-1] >= 0) {
                    dp[j] += dp[j-nums[i-1]];
                }
            }
        }
        return dp[target];
    }
}
```

- Recognizing as subset sum problem avoids exponential backtracking; DP is O(n*sum).

---

#### #446. Arithmetic Slices II - Subsequence

[leetcode.com/problems/arithmetic-slices-ii-subsequence/](https://leetcode.com/problems/arithmetic-slices-ii-subsequence/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Count the number of arithmetic slices that are subsequences of an array.

**Example:** `nums = [2,4,6,8,10], k = 2` → `8`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    //  f[i][d]  nums[i] d
    //     d=nums[i]−nums[j]
    //     nums[i]  nums[j]  d
    public int numberOfArithmeticSlices(int[] nums) {
        int result = 0;
        int n = nums.length;
        // d  map
        // array
        Map<Long, Integer>[] f = new Map[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            f[i] = new HashMap<>();
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < i; ++j) {
                long d = 1L * nums[i] - nums[j];
                int count = f[j].getOrDefault(d, 0);
                result += count;
                f[i].put(d, f[i].getOrDefault(d, 0) + count + 1);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #552. Student Attendance Record II

[leetcode.com/problems/student-attendance-record-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/student-attendance-record-ii/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Count valid attendance records of length n (at most 1 absence, never 3+ consecutive lates).

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  3  3
    public int checkRecord(int n) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[][][] dp = new int[n + 1][2][3]; // A  L
        dp[0][0][0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            //  P
            for (int j = 0; j <= 1; j++) {
                for (int k = 0; k <= 2; k++) {
                    dp[i][j][0] = (dp[i][j][0] + dp[i - 1][j][k]) % MOD;
                }
            }
            //  A
            for (int k = 0; k <= 2; k++) {
                dp[i][1][0] = (dp[i][1][0] + dp[i - 1][0][k]) % MOD;
            }
            //  L
            for (int j = 0; j <= 1; j++) {
                for (int k = 1; k <= 2; k++) {
                    dp[i][j][k] = (dp[i][j][k] + dp[i - 1][j][k - 1]) % MOD;
                }
            }
        }
        int sum = 0;
        for (int j = 0; j <= 1; j++) {
            for (int k = 0; k <= 2; k++) {
                sum = (sum + dp[n][j][k]) % MOD;
            }
        }
        return sum;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #576. Out of Boundary Paths

[leetcode.com/problems/out-of-boundary-paths/](https://leetcode.com/problems/out-of-boundary-paths/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Count paths from a given cell that leave an m×n grid in exactly maxMove steps.

**Example:** `m = 2, n = 2, maxMove = 2, startRow = 0, startColumn = 0` → `6`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    int[][] dirs = new int[][] {{0, 1}, {0, -1}, {1, 0}, {-1, 0}};
    //
    int MOD = 1000000007;
    public int findPaths(int m, int n, int maxMove, int startRow, int startColumn) {
        //
        int[][][] memo = new int[m][n][maxMove + 1];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
                for (int k = 0; k <= maxMove; k++) {
                    memo[i][j][k] = -1;
                }
            }
        }
        return dfs(m, n, maxMove, startRow, startColumn, memo);
    }
    private int dfs(int m, int n, int moveCount, int i, int j, int[][][] memo) {
        //
        if (i < 0 || j < 0 || i >= m || j >= n) {
            return 1;
        }
        // 0
        if (moveCount == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        if (memo[i][j][moveCount] != -1) {
            return memo[i][j][moveCount];
        }
        //
        if (i - moveCount >= 0 && j - moveCount >= 0 && i + moveCount < m && j + moveCount < n) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        int sum = 0;
        for (int[] dir : dirs) {
            //
            sum = (sum + dfs(m, n, moveCount - 1, i + dir[0], j + dir[1], memo)) % MOD;
        }
        //
        memo[i][j][moveCount] = sum;
        return sum;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int findPaths(int m, int n, int maxMove, int startRow, int startColumn) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[][] directions = {{-1, 0}, {1, 0}, {0, -1}, {0, 1}};
        int outCounts = 0;
        // dp[i][j][k]  i (j,k)
        int[][][] dp = new int[maxMove + 1][m][n];
        dp[0][startRow][startColumn] = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < maxMove; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                for (int k = 0; k < n; k++) {
                    int count = dp[i][j][k];
                    if (count > 0) {
                        for (int[] direction : directions) {
                            int j1 = j + direction[0], k1 = k + direction[1];
                            if (j1 >= 0 && j1 < m && k1 >= 0 && k1 < n) {
                                //  i + 1
                                dp[i + 1][j1][k1] = (dp[i + 1][j1][k1] + dp[i][j][k]) % MOD;
                            } else {
                                //  i + 1  dp[i][j][k]
                                outCounts = (outCounts + dp[i][j][k]) % MOD;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return outCounts;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int findPaths(int m, int n, int maxMove, int startRow, int startColumn) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[][] directions = {{-1, 0}, {1, 0}, {0, -1}, {0, 1}};
        int outCounts = 0;
        int[][] dp = new int[m][n];
        dp[startRow][startColumn] = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < maxMove; i++) {
            int[][] dpNew = new int[m][n];
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                for (int k = 0; k < n; k++) {
                    int count = dp[j][k];
                    if (count > 0) {
                        for (int[] direction : directions) {
                            int j1 = j + direction[0], k1 = k + direction[1];
                            if (j1 >= 0 && j1 < m && k1 >= 0 && k1 < n) {
                                dpNew[j1][k1] = (dpNew[j1][k1] + count) % MOD;
                            } else {
                                outCounts = (outCounts + count) % MOD;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            dp = dpNew;
        }
        return outCounts;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #600. Non-negative Integers without Consecutive Ones

[leetcode.com/problems/nonnegative-integers-without-consecutive-ones/](https://leetcode.com/problems/nonnegative-integers-without-consecutive-ones/) · `O(n²) / O(n)`

**Example:** `n = 5` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n²) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  DP [a, b]a  b  a < b [0, x]  int dp(int x) [a, b] dp(b) - dp(a - 1)
    static int N = 50;
    // f[i][j]  i j0 or 1
    //  f[2][1]  2 1  3 100100
    static int[][] f = new int[N][2];
    static {
        f[1][0] = 1; f[1][1] = 2;
        for (int i = 1; i < N - 1; i++) {
            f[i + 1][0] = f[i][1];
            f[i + 1][1] = f[i][0] + f[i][1];
        }
    }
    private int getHighBit(int n) {
        for (int i = 31; i >= 0; i--) {
            if((n & (1 << i)) != 0) return i;
            // if (((n >> i) & 1) == 1) return i;
        }
        return 0;
    }
    public int findIntegers(int n) {
        int len = getHighBit(n);
        int result = 0, previous = 0;
        for (int i = len; i >= 0; i--) {
            //  0  1
            int current = ((n >> i) & 1);
            //  1  0  f[i + 1][0]
            if (current == 1) result += f[i + 1][0];
            //  1
            if (previous == 1 && current == 1) break;
            previous = current;
            if (i == 0) result++;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
LCP 09.
class Solution {
    public int minJump(int[] jump) {
        int n = jump.length;
        boolean[] visited = new boolean[n];
        Deque<Integer> queue = new ArrayDeque<>();
        queue.offer(0);
        visited[0] = true;
        int leftBound = 0;
        for(int iter = 1; !queue.isEmpty(); iter++) {
            for(int size = queue.size(); size > 0; size--) {
                int current = queue.poll();
                if(current + jump[current] >= n) {
                    return iter;
                }
                if(current + jump[current] < n && !visited[current + jump[current]]) {
                    visited[current + jump[current]] = true;
                    queue.offer(current + jump[current]);
                }
                for(int i = leftBound; i < current; i++) {
                    if(!visited[i]) {
                        visited[i] = true;
                        queue.offer(i);
                    }
                }
                leftBound = current + 1;
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int minJump(int[] jump) {
        int len = jump.length;
        //dp[]
        int[] dp = new int[len];
        //dp[]
        for (int i = len - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            dp[i] = i + jump[i] >= len ? 1 : dp[i + jump[i]] + 1;
            for (int j = i + 1; j < len && j < i + jump[i] && dp[j] > dp[i]; j++) {
                dp[j] = Math.min(dp[j], dp[i] + 1);
            }
        }
        return dp[0];
    }
}
Offer 37.   LCOF
public class Codec {
    private String DELIMITER = ",";
    // Encodes a tree to a single string.
    public String serialize(TreeNode root) {
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        dfs(root, sb);
        return sb.toString();
    }
    private void dfs(TreeNode root, StringBuilder sb) {
        if(root == null) {
            return;
        }
        sb.append(root.val).append(DELIMITER);
        dfs(root.left, sb);
        dfs(root.right, sb);
    }
    // Decodes your encoded data to tree.
    public TreeNode deserialize(String data) {
        if(data.isEmpty()) {
            return null;
        }
        String[] splits = data.split(DELIMITER);
        Queue<String> queue = new LinkedList<>();
        for(String split: splits) {
            queue.offer(split);
        }
        return buildTree(queue, Integer.MIN_VALUE, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
    }
    private TreeNode buildTree(Queue<String> queue, int lower, int upper) {
        if(queue.isEmpty()) {
            return null;
        }
        // peekpoll
        String head = queue.peek();
        int val = Integer.parseInt(head);
        if(val < lower || val > upper) {
            return null;
        }
        // pollbuild nodenode
        queue.poll();
        TreeNode root = new TreeNode(val);
        root.left = buildTree(queue, lower, val);
        root.right = buildTree(queue, val, upper);
        return root;
    }
}
// Your Codec object will be instantiated and called as such:
// Codec codec = new Codec();
// codec.deserialize(codec.serialize(root));
public class Codec {
    private final String DELINIMATOR = "#";
    private final String NULL = "null";
    public String serialize(TreeNode root) {
        if(root == null){
            return NULL;
        }
        return root.val + DELINIMATOR + serialize(root.left) + DELINIMATOR + serialize(root.right);
    }
    // Decodes your encoded data to tree.
    public TreeNode deserialize(String data) {
        System.out.println(data);
        Deque<String> queue = new ArrayDeque<>(Arrays.asList(data.split(DELINIMATOR)));
        return dfs(queue);
    }
    private TreeNode dfs(Deque<String> queue) {
        String currentVal = queue.poll();
        if(currentVal.equals(NULL)){
            return null;
        }
        TreeNode root = new TreeNode(Integer.parseInt(currentVal));
        root.left = dfs(queue);
        root.right = dfs(queue);
        return root;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #629. K Inverse Pairs Array

[leetcode.com/problems/k-inverse-pairs-array/](https://leetcode.com/problems/k-inverse-pairs-array/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Count permutations of 1..n with exactly k inverse pairs.

**Example:** `n = 3, k = 0` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    // dp[i][j]  i  j
    // dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i-1][j-1] + ... + dp[i-1][j-i-1]
    // dp[1][0]=1
    // dp[n][k]
    int MOD = 1000000007;
    public int kInversePairs(int n, int k) {
        // dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i-1][j-1] + ... + dp[i-1][j-i-1]
        // dp[i][j-1] =            dp[i-1][j-1] + ... + dp[i-1][j-1-(i-1-1)] + dp[i-1][j-1-i-1]
        // dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] - dp[i-1][j-1-i-1]
        long[][] dp = new long[n + 1][k + 1];
        dp[1][0] = 1;
        for (int i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j <= Math.min(k, i * (i - 1) / 2); j++) {
                // dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] - dp[i-1][j-1-i-1]
                dp[i][j] = ((j >= 1 ? dp[i][j - 1] : 0) + dp[i - 1][j] - (j >= i ? dp[i - 1][j - i] : 0) + MOD) % MOD;
            }
        }
        return (int) dp[n][k];
    }
}
```

- Use two variables instead of array if only last 1-2 states needed — O(1) space.

#### #638. Shopping Offers

[leetcode.com/problems/shopping-offers/](https://leetcode.com/problems/shopping-offers/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Minimize the cost to purchase items given special-offer bundles.

**Example:** `price = [2,5], special = [[3,0,5],[1,2,10]], needs = [3,2]` → `14`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    int result = 0x3f3f3f3f;
    List<Integer> price, needs;
    List<List<Integer>> special;
    Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
    int n, m;
    public int shoppingOffers(List<Integer> _price, List<List<Integer>> _special, List<Integer> _needs) {
        price = _price; special = _special; needs = _needs;
        n = price.size();
        List<Integer> temp = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) temp.add(0);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            List<Integer> clone = new ArrayList<>(temp);
            clone.set(i, 1);
            clone.add(price.get(i));
            special.add(clone);
        }
        m = special.size();
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
            List<Integer> x = special.get(i);
            int k = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
                int a = x.get(j), b = needs.get(j);
                if (a == 0 || b == 0) continue;
                if (a > b) {
                    k = 0;
                    break;
                }
                k = k == 0 ? b / a : Math.min(k, b / a);
            }
            map.put(i, k);
        }
        dfs(0, needs, 0);
        return result;
    }
    void dfs(int u, List<Integer> list, int current) {
        if (current >= result) return ;
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) count += list.get(i);
        if (count == 0) {
            result = current;
            return ;
        }
        if (u == m) return;
        List<Integer> x = special.get(u);
        out:for (int k = 0; k <= map.get(u); k++) {
            List<Integer> clist = new ArrayList<>(list);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                int a = x.get(i), b = clist.get(i);
                if (a * k > b) break out;
                clist.set(i, b - a * k);
            }
            dfs(u + 1, clist, current + k * x.get(n));
        }
    }
}
```

- Memoization (top-down) is easier to write; tabulation (bottom-up) avoids recursion overhead.
- Identify if problem has only O(n) distinct states — may allow O(n) DP instead of O(n²).

---

#### #650. 2 Keys Keyboard

[leetcode.com/problems/2-keys-keyboard/](https://leetcode.com/problems/2-keys-keyboard/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find minimum steps to get exactly n 'A's on a screen using only Copy All and Paste.

**Example:** `n = 3` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    // f[i][j]  i  j
    int INF = 0x3f3f3f3f;
    public int minSteps(int n) {
        int[][] f = new int[n + 1][n + 1];
        for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j <= n; j++) {
                f[i][j] = INF;
            }
        }
        f[1][0] = 0; f[1][1] = 1;
        for (int i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
            int min = INF;
            for (int j = 0; j <= i / 2; j++) {
                f[i][j] = f[i - j][j] + 1;
                min = Math.min(min, f[i][j]);
            }
            f[i][i] = min + 1;
        }
        int result = INF;
        for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) result = Math.min(result, f[n][i]);
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    // 1  Copy All + x  Paste“”
    public int minSteps(int n) {
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) {
            while (n % i == 0) {
                result += i;
                n /= i;
            }
        }
        if (n != 1) result += n;
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #674. Longest Continuous Increasing Subsequence

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-continuous-increasing-subsequence/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-continuous-increasing-subsequence/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Example:** `nums = [1,3,5,4,7,8,9,10,11,15]` → `6`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findLengthOfLCIS(int[] nums) {
        int max = 1;
        int i = 0;
        while(i < nums.length) {
            int j = i;
            while(j + 1 < nums.length && nums[j + 1] > nums[j]) {
                j++;
            }
            max = Math.max(max, j - i + 1);
            i = j + 1;
        }
        return max;
    }
}
```

- Memoization (top-down) is easier to write; tabulation (bottom-up) avoids recursion overhead.
- Identify if problem has only O(n) distinct states — may allow O(n) DP instead of O(n²).

---

#### #688. Knight Probability in Chessboard

[leetcode.com/problems/knight-probability-in-chessboard/](https://leetcode.com/problems/knight-probability-in-chessboard/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the probability that a knight stays on an n×n board after exactly k moves.

**Example:** `n = 3, k = 2, row = 0, column = 0` → `0.06250`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define recursive function with memoization cache.
2. Check memo before computing: return cached result if present.
3. Recursively solve subproblems and combine.
4. Store result in memo before returning.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    private int[][] directions = new int[][]{{1, 2}, {2, 1}, {2, -1}, {1, -2}, {-1, -2}, {-2, -1}, {-2, 1}, {-1, 2}};
    //  f[r][c][steps]  (r, c)  steps
    private double[][][] memo = new double[26][26][101];
    public double knightProbability(int n, int k, int row, int column) {
        if(row < 0 || row >= n || column <  0 || column >= n) {
            return 0;
        }
        if(k == 0) {
            return 1;
        }
        if(memo[row][column][k] != 0) {
            return memo[row][column][k];
        }
        double sum = 0;
        for(int[] direction : directions) {
            int x = row + direction[0];
            int y = column + direction[1];
            sum += knightProbability(n, k-1, x, y);
        }
        memo[row][column][k] = sum / 8;
        return memo[row][column][k];
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #718. Maximum Length of Repeated Subarray

[leetcode.com/problems/maximum-length-of-repeated-subarray/](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-length-of-repeated-subarray/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the maximum length of a subarray that appears in both arrays.

**Example:** `nums1 = [1,2,3,2,1], nums2 = [3,2,1,4,7]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    int mod = 1000000009;
    int base = 113;
    public int findLength(int[] A, int[] B) {
        int left = 1, right = Math.min(A.length, B.length) + 1;
        while (left < right) {
            int mid = (left + right) >> 1;
            if (check(A, B, mid)) {
                left = mid + 1;
            } else {
                right = mid;
            }
        }
        return left - 1;
    }
    public boolean check(int[] A, int[] B, int len) {
        long hashA = 0;
        //  len
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            hashA = (hashA * base + A[i]) % mod;
        }
        Set<Long> bucketA = new HashSet<>();
        bucketA.add(hashA);
        long mult = quickPow(base, len - 1);
        for (int i = len; i < A.length; i++) {
            hashA = ((hashA - A[i - len] * mult % mod + mod) % mod * base + A[i]) % mod;
            bucketA.add(hashA);
        }
        //  len
        long hashB = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            hashB = (hashB * base + B[i]) % mod;
        }
        if (bucketA.contains(hashB)) {
            return true;
        }
        for (int i = len; i < B.length; i++) {
            hashB = ((hashB - B[i - len] * mult % mod + mod) % mod * base + B[i]) % mod;
            if (bucketA.contains(hashB)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    //  x^n % mod
    public long quickPow(long x, long n) {
        long ret = 1;
        while (n != 0) {
            if ((n & 1) != 0) {
                ret = ret * x % mod;
            }
            x = x * x % mod;
            n >>= 1;
        }
        return ret;
    }
}
```

- Memoization (top-down) is easier to write; tabulation (bottom-up) avoids recursion overhead.
- Identify if problem has only O(n) distinct states — may allow O(n) DP instead of O(n²).

---

#### #740. Delete and Earn

[leetcode.com/problems/delete-and-earn/](https://leetcode.com/problems/delete-and-earn/) · `O(n²) / O(n)`

**Q:** Delete and earn: delete nums[i] to earn nums[i] points, but must delete all adjacent values.

**Example:** `nums = [3,4,2], points = [0,0,0,3,4,0,...](by index)` → `6`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n²) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  x  x−1  x+1  x  x−1  x+1  x  x x
    //   nums
    public int deleteAndEarn(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int max = nums[0];
        for(int num : nums) {
            max = Math.max(max, num);
        }
        int[] dp = new int[max + 1];
        for(int num : nums) {
            dp[num] += num;
        }
        int first = dp[0], second = Math.max(dp[0], dp[1]);
        for(int i = 2; i <= max; i++) {
            int temp = second;
            second = Math.max(second, dp[i] + first);
            first = temp;
        }
        return second;
    }
}
```

- Use two variables instead of array if only last 1-2 states needed — O(1) space.

---

#### #787. Cheapest Flights Within K Stops

[leetcode.com/problems/cheapest-flights-within-k-stops/](https://leetcode.com/problems/cheapest-flights-within-k-stops/) · `O(k*E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the cheapest flight from src to dst with at most k stops.

**Example:** `n = 3, flights = [[0,1,100],[1,2,100],[0,2,500]], src = 0, dst = 2, k = 1` → `200`

**Algorithm:**
1. Bellman-Ford with k+1 rounds.
2. dp[i] = min cost to reach node i.
3. For each round: update dp[dst] = min(dp[dst], dp[src]+cost) for each flight.
4. Use copy of dp to avoid using updated values in same round.
`O(k*E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  dp[t][i]  t  src  i
    //  jj t-1  f[t−1][j]  cost(j,i)  f[t][i]
    public int findCheapestPrice(int n, int[][] flights, int src, int dst, int k) {
        final int INF = 10000 * 101 + 1;
        int[][] dp = new int[k + 2][n];
        for (int i = 0; i < k + 2; ++i) {
            Arrays.fill(dp[i], INF);
        }
        dp[0][src] = 0;
        for (int t = 1; t <= k + 1; t++) {
            for (int[] flight : flights) {
                int j = flight[0], i = flight[1], cost = flight[2];
                dp[t][i] = Math.min(dp[t][i], dp[t - 1][j] + cost);
            }
        }
        int result = INF;
        for (int t = 1; t <= k + 1; t++) {
            result = Math.min(result, dp[t][dst]);
        }
        return result == INF ? -1 : result;
    }
}
```

- DP (Bellman-Ford k+1 rounds) is cleaner than modified Dijkstra for this problem.

---

#### #873. Length of Longest Fibonacci Subsequence

[leetcode.com/problems/length-of-longest-fibonacci-subsequence/](https://leetcode.com/problems/length-of-longest-fibonacci-subsequence/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Find the length of the longest Fibonacci-like subsequence in a sorted array.

**Example:** `nums = [1,2,1,2,3,5,1,1]` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int lenLongestFibSubseq(int[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;
        int max = 0;
        int[][] dp = new int[n][n];
        for (int end = 2; end < n; end++) {
            int L = 0, R = end - 1;
            while (L < R) {
                if (arr[L] + arr[R] == arr[end]) {
                    if (dp[L][R] == 0) {
                        dp[L][R] = 2;
                        dp[R][end] = 3;
                    } else {
                        dp[R][end] = dp[L][R] + 1;
                    }
                    max = Math.max(max, dp[R][end]);
                    L++;
                    R--;
                } else if (arr[L] + arr[R] < arr[end]) {
                    L++;
                } else {
                    R--;
                }
            }
        }
        return max;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #887. Super Egg Drop

[leetcode.com/problems/super-egg-drop/](https://leetcode.com/problems/super-egg-drop/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Find the minimum number of moves to determine the critical floor for egg drops.

**Example:** `k = 1, n = 2` → `14`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int superEggDrop(int k, int n) {
        if(n == 1) return 1;
        // dp[n][k]: kn
        int[][] dp = new int[n+1][k+1];
        for(int i = 1; i <= k; i++) {
            dp[1][i] = 1;
        }
        for(int i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
            for(int j = 1; j <= k; j++) {
                //
                dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i-1][j-1] + dp[i-1][j];
                if(dp[i][j] >= n) {
                    return i;
                }
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
class Solution {
    int[][] memo;
    public int superEggDrop(int k, int n) {
        memo = new int[n+1][k+1];
        // dp[n][k]: nk
        return dp(n, k);
    }
    public int dp(int n, int k){
        // base case
        // (n == 0)
        // (k == 1)112...nn
        if(n == 0){
            return 0;
        }
        if(k == 1){
            return n;
        }
        //
        if(memo[n][k] != 0){
            return memo[n][k];
        }
        int result = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        //
        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++){
            result = Math.min(result, Math.max(dp(i-1, k-1), dp(n-i, k)) + 1);
        }
        memo[n][k] = result;
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #918. Maximum Sum Circular Subarray

[leetcode.com/problems/maximum-sum-circular-subarray/](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-sum-circular-subarray/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Example:** `nums = [1,-2,3,-2]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int maxSubarraySumCircular(int[] A) {
        int total = 0, maxSum = A[0], curMax = 0, minSum = A[0], curMin = 0;
        for (int a : A) {
            curMax = Math.max(curMax + a, a);
            maxSum = Math.max(maxSum, curMax);
            curMin = Math.min(curMin + a, a);
            minSum = Math.min(minSum, curMin);
            total += a;
        }
        return maxSum > 0 ? Math.max(maxSum, total - minSum) : maxSum;
    }
}
```

- Memoization (top-down) is easier to write; tabulation (bottom-up) avoids recursion overhead.
- Identify if problem has only O(n) distinct states — may allow O(n) DP instead of O(n²).

#### #935. Knight Dialer

[leetcode.com/problems/knight-dialer/](https://leetcode.com/problems/knight-dialer/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Count distinct phone numbers of length n that a knight can dial.

**Example:** `n = 2` → `16`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
/**
*
* 0 -> 4, 6
* 1 -> 6, 8
* 2 -> 7, 9
* 3 -> 4, 8
* 4 -> 3, 9, 0
* 5 ->
* 6 -> 1, 7, 0
* 7 -> 2, 6
* 8 -> 1, 3
* 9 -> 4, 2
*  dp[time][num] timenum
*  timenum,time-1num
*  dp[time][num]time-1numdp
*  dp[N-1][]
*/
public class Solution {
    //num,
    private int[][] path = {{4, 6}, {6, 8}, {7, 9}, {4, 8}, {3, 9, 0}, {}, {1, 7, 0}, {2, 6}, {1, 3}, {4, 2}};
    private static final int MOD = 1000000007;
    public int knightDialer(int N) {
        int[][] dp = new int[N][10];
        for (int num = 0; num < 10; num++)
        dp[0][num] = 1; //0num1
        for (int time = 1; time < N; time++) {
            for (int num = 0; num < 10; num++){
                for (int arrive : path[num])
                dp[time][num] = (dp[time][num] + dp[time - 1][arrive]) % MOD;
            }
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int num = 0; num < 10; num++)
        result = (result + dp[N - 1][num]) % MOD;
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

#### #983. Minimum Cost For Tickets

[leetcode.com/problems/minimum-cost-for-tickets/](https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-cost-for-tickets/) · `O(n²) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the minimum cost to cover all travel days using 1-, 7-, or 30-day passes.

**Example:** `costs = [[1,7,2],[3,6,3],[12,3,1],[5,9,2]], days = [1,4,6,7,8,20]` → `11`

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n²) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int mincostTickets(int[] days, int[] costs) {
        int n = days.length;
        int[] dp = new int[n+31];
        Set<Integer> set = new HashSet<>();
        for(int day : days) {
            set.add(day);
        }
        for(int i = n; i >= 0; i--) {
            if(set.contains(i)) {
                dp[i] = Math.min(dp[i+1] + costs[0], dp[i+7] + costs[1]);
                dp[i] = Math.min(dp[i], dp[i+30] + costs[2]);
            } else {
                dp[i] = dp[i + 1];
            }
        }
        return dp[0];
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int mincostTickets(int[] days, int[] costs) {
        int len = days.length;
        int last = days[len-1];//
        int[] dp = new int[last+1];//dp[i]i
        int pos = 0;
        for (int i=1;i<=last;i++){
            if (i==days[pos]){
                int day1 = i-1;
                int day2 = i-7 > 0? i-7 : 0;
                int day3 = i-30 > 0 ? i-30 : 0;
                dp[i] = Math.min(dp[day1]+costs[0],Math.min(dp[day2]+costs[1],dp[day3]+costs[2]));
                pos++;
            }else{
                dp[i] = dp[i-1];
            }
        }
        return dp[last];
    }
}
```

- Use two variables instead of array if only last 1-2 states needed — O(1) space.

---

#### #1027. Longest Arithmetic Subsequence

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-arithmetic-subsequence/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-arithmetic-subsequence/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Find the length of the longest arithmetic subsequence in an array.

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i][j] = solution for subproblem on s1[0..i-1], s2[0..j-1].
2. Set base cases: dp[0][j] and dp[i][0].
3. Fill row by row: if chars match → dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else take max/min of neighbors.
4. Return dp[m][n].
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int longestArithSeqLength(int[] A) {
        int n = A.length;
        if(n == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        Map<Integer, Map<Integer, Integer>> map = new HashMap<>(); //dp
        int result = 1;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            map.put(i, new HashMap<>());
            for(int j = i - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                //
                int diff = A[i] - A[j];
                if(map.get(i).containsKey(diff)) {
                    continue;
                }
                //
                int len =  map.get(j).getOrDefault(diff, 0);
                //
                result = Math.max(result, len + 2);
                //
                map.get(i).put(diff, len + 1);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int longestArithSeqLength(int[] A) {
        if(A == null || A.length < 3)
        return 0;
        int[][] dp = new int[A.length][20001];
        int max = 0;
        for(int i = 1; i < A.length; i++){
            for(int j = 0; j < i; j++){
                int distance = A[i] - A[j] + 10000; //offset
                if(dp[j][distance] > 0){
                    dp[i][distance] = Math.max(dp[i][distance], dp[j][distance]+1);
                }
                else{
                    dp[i][distance] = 2;
                }
                max = max > dp[i][distance] ? max : dp[i][distance];
            }
        }
        return max;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #1048. Longest String Chain

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-string-chain/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-string-chain/) · `O(n²) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the longest string chain where each word is a predecessor of the next.

**Algorithm:**
1. Define dp[i] = answer for subproblem of size i.
2. Set base case dp[0] (and dp[1] if needed).
3. Fill dp left to right with recurrence: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...).
4. Return dp[n] or max/min over dp array.
`O(n²) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int longestStrChain(String[] words) {
        Map<String, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        int longest = 1;
        Arrays.sort(words, (a, b) -> a.length() - b.length());
        for(String word : words) {
            int best = 1;
            for(int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) {
                String predecessor = word.substring(0, i) + word.substring(i+1);
                int len = map.getOrDefault(predecessor, 0);
                best = Math.max(best, len + 1);
            }
            map.put(word, best);
            longest = Math.max(longest, best);
        }
        return longest;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int longestStrChain(String[] words) {
        Arrays.sort(words,(a, b) -> (a.length() - b.length()));
        int n = words.length;
        int[] dp=new int[n];
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++){
            for(int j = i+1; j < n; j++){
                if(isPredecessor(words[i], words[j])){
                    dp[j] = Math.max(dp[i] + 1, dp[j]);
                }
            }
        }
        int result=0;
        for(int i:dp)result=Math.max(result,i);
        return result+1;
    }
    public boolean isPredecessor(String a,String b){
        if(b.length() - a.length() !=1)return false;
        int count = 0;
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while(i < a.length() && j < b.length()){
            if(a.charAt(i) == b.charAt(j)){
                i++;
                j++;
            }else{
                count++;
                j++;
                if(count > 1) {
                    return false;
                }
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Use two variables instead of array if only last 1-2 states needed — O(1) space.
- After sorting, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of linear scan for lookups.
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.
