#### #1136. Parallel Courses

[leetcode.com/problems/parallel-courses/](https://leetcode.com/problems/parallel-courses/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the minimum number of months to complete all courses given prerequisites and durations.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from edges.
2. Init queue with source; mark visited.
3. BFS: poll node, process, enqueue unvisited neighbors.
4. Track levels for shortest path; track visited for components.
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int minimumSemesters(int n, int[][] relations) {
        if(relations == null || relations.length == 0 || relations[0].length == 0) {
            return 1;
        }
        List<Integer>[] graph = buildGraph(n, relations);
        int[] inDegree = new int[n+1];
        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            for(int j : graph[i]) {
                inDegree[j]++;
            }
        }
        Deque<Integer> queue = new ArrayDeque<>();
        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            if(inDegree[i] == 0) {
                queue.offer(i);
            }
        }
        int count = 0;
        int i;
        for(i = 0; !queue.isEmpty(); i++) {
            for(int j = queue.size(); j > 0; j--) {
                int current = queue.poll();
                count++;
                for(int neighbor : graph[current]) {
                    inDegree[neighbor]--;
                    if(inDegree[neighbor] == 0) {
                        queue.offer(neighbor);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return count == n ? i : -1;
    }
    public List<Integer>[] buildGraph(int n, int[][] relations) {
        List<Integer>[] graph = new List[n+1];
        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            graph[i] = new ArrayList<>();
        }
        for(int[] relation : relations) {
            graph[relation[0]].add(relation[1]);
        }
        return graph;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1162. As Far from Land as Possible

[leetcode.com/problems/as-far-from-land-as-possible/](https://leetcode.com/problems/as-far-from-land-as-possible/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from edges.
2. Init queue with source; mark visited.
3. BFS: poll node, process, enqueue unvisited neighbors.
4. Track levels for shortest path; track visited for components.
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    static int[] dx = {-1, 0, 1, 0};
    static int[] dy = {0, 1, 0, -1};
    int n;
    int[][] grid;
    public int maxDistance(int[][] grid) {
        this.n = grid.length;
        this.grid = grid;
        int result = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; ++j) {
                if (grid[i][j] == 0) {
                    result = Math.max(result, findNearestLand(i, j));
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
    public int findNearestLand(int x, int y) {
        boolean[][] vis = new boolean[n][n];
        Queue<int[]> queue = new LinkedList<>();
        queue.offer(new int[]{x, y, 0});
        vis[x][y] = true;
        while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
            int[] f = queue.poll();
            for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
                int nx = f[0] + dx[i], ny = f[1] + dy[i];
                if (!(nx >= 0 && nx < n && ny >= 0 && ny < n)) {
                    continue;
                }
                if (!vis[nx][ny]) {
                    queue.offer(new int[]{nx, ny, f[2] + 1});
                    vis[nx][ny] = true;
                    if (grid[nx][ny] == 1) {
                        return f[2] + 1;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1197. Minimum Knight Moves

[leetcode.com/problems/minimum-knight-moves/](https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-knight-moves/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the minimum knight moves from origin to a target on an infinite board.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from input edges.
2. DFS from each unvisited node; mark visited before recursing.
3. Detect cycles: node in current DFS path → cycle found.
4. Collect result (component count, topological order, etc.).
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    private int mov[][] = {{-2,1},{2,1},{2,-1},{-2,-1},
        {1,-2},{1,2},{-1,2},{-1,-2}};
    public int minKnightMoves(int x, int y) {
        Deque<int[]> queue0 = new ArrayDeque<>();
        Deque<int[]> queue1 = new ArrayDeque<>();
        HashMap<Integer, Integer> map0 = new HashMap<>();	//
        HashMap<Integer, Integer> map1 = new HashMap<>();	//
        queue0.add(new int[] {0,0});
        queue1.add(new int[] {x,y});
        map0.put(0, 0);
        map1.put(x * 1000 + y, 0); 	//x*1000+yhashcode
        while (map0.size() > 0 || map1.size() > 0) {
            if(map0.size() <= map1.size()) {
                int siz = queue0.size();
                while (siz-- > 0) {
                    int current[] = queue0.poll();
                    int curHash = current[0] * 1000 + current[1];
                    int curStep = map0.get(curHash);
                    if (map1.containsKey(curHash)) {
                        return map0.get(curHash) + map1.get(curHash);
                    }
                    for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) {
                        int nx = current[0] + mov[i][0], ny = current[1] + mov[i][1];
                        int nHash = nx  * 1000 + ny;
                        if (map0.containsKey(nHash)) {
                            continue;
                        }
                        queue0.add(new int[]{nx, ny});
                        map0.put(nHash, curStep + 1);
                    }
                }
            } else {
                int siz = queue1.size();
                while (siz-- > 0) {
                    int current[] = queue1.poll();
                    int curHash = current[0] * 1000 + current[1];
                    int curStep = map1.get(curHash);
                    if (map0.containsKey(curHash)) {
                        return map0.get(curHash) + map1.get(curHash);
                    }
                    for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) {
                        int nx = current[0] + mov[i][0], ny = current[1] + mov[i][1];
                        int nHash = nx  * 1000 + ny;
                        if (map1.containsKey(nHash)) {
                            continue;
                        }
                        queue1.add(new int[]{nx, ny});
                        map1.put(nHash, curStep + 1);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #1202. Smallest String With Swaps

[leetcode.com/problems/smallest-string-with-swaps/](https://leetcode.com/problems/smallest-string-with-swaps/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Swap characters at given index pairs to get the lexicographically smallest string.

**Algorithm:**
1. Union-Find: each swap pair merges two indices into same component. Indices in the same component can be freely rearranged. Sort characters in each component, assign in sorted order.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    int[] parent;
    public String smallestStringWithSwaps(String s, List<List<Integer>> pairs) {
        int n = s.length();
        parent = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) parent[i] = i;
        for (List<Integer> pair : pairs) union(pair.get(0), pair.get(1));
        Map<Integer, List<Character>> groups = new HashMap<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        groups.computeIfAbsent(find(i), k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(s.charAt(i));
        for (List<Character> chars : groups.values()) Collections.sort(chars);
        Map<Integer, Integer> pointer = new HashMap<>();
        char[] result = new char[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            int root = find(i);
            int index = pointer.getOrDefault(root, 0);
            result[i] = groups.get(root).get(index);  // take next sorted char in group
            pointer.put(root, index + 1);
        }
        return new String(result);
    }
    int find(int x) { return parent[x] == x ? x : (parent[x] = find(parent[x])); }
    void union(int x, int y) { parent[find(x)] = find(y); }
}
```

- Use rank-based union to keep tree flat and avoid O(n) find chains.
- Sort characters in-place per group using a TreeMap keyed by root.

---

#### #1245. Tree Diameter

[leetcode.com/problems/tree-diameter/](https://leetcode.com/problems/tree-diameter/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the diameter of an N-ary tree (longest path between any two nodes).

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from input edges.
2. DFS from each unvisited node; mark visited before recursing.
3. Detect cycles: node in current DFS path → cycle found.
4. Collect result (component count, topological order, etc.).
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    int result = 0;
    public int treeDiameter(int[][] edges) {
        List<Integer>[] graph = buildGraph(edges);
        boolean[] visited = new boolean[edges.length+1];
        dfs(graph,0, visited);
        return result;
    }
    private List<Integer>[] buildGraph(int[][] edges) {
        List<Integer>[] graph = new ArrayList[edges.length+1];
        for(int i=0 ; i< graph.length ; i++){
            graph[i] = new ArrayList<>();
        }
        for(int[] edge : edges){
            graph[edge[0]].add(edge[1]);
            graph[edge[1]].add(edge[0]);
        }
        return graph;
    }
    private int dfs(List<Integer>[] graph,int index,boolean[] visited){
        visited[index] = true;
        int max1 = 0;
        int max2 = 0;
        for(int next : graph[index]){
            if(!visited[next]){
                int num = dfs(graph,next,visited);
                if(num > max1){
                    max2 = max1;
                    max1 = num;
                } else if(num > max2){
                    max2 = num;
                }
            }
        }
        result = Math.max(result,max1 + max2);
        return Math.max(max1, max2)+1;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1293. Shortest Path in a Grid with Obstacles Elimination

[leetcode.com/problems/shortest-path-in-a-grid-with-obstacles-elimination/](https://leetcode.com/problems/shortest-path-in-a-grid-with-obstacles-elimination/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the shortest path from start to end in a grid, eliminating at most k obstacles.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from edges.
2. Init queue with source; mark visited.
3. BFS: poll node, process, enqueue unvisited neighbors.
4. Track levels for shortest path; track visited for components.
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int shortestPath(int[][] grid, int k) {
        int row = grid.length, col = grid[0].length;
        if (row == 1 && col == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        // direction matrix
        int[][] dirs = new int[][]{{0, 1}, {0, -1}, {1, 0}, {-1, 0}};
        Deque<int[]> queue = new LinkedList<>();
        int[][] visited = new int[row][col];
        for (int i = 0; i < row; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < col; ++j) {
                visited[i][j] = -1;
            }
        }
        queue.offerLast(new int[]{0, 0 ,k});
        visited[0][0] = k;
        int step = 0;
        while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
            int currSize = queue.size();
            step++;
            for (int i = 0; i < currSize; ++i) {
                int[] currPos = queue.pollFirst();
                for (int j = 0; j < 4; ++j) {
                    int nx = currPos[0] + dirs[j][0];
                    int ny = currPos[1] + dirs[j][1];
                    int currK = currPos[2];
                    //
                    if (nx >= 0 && nx < row && ny >= 0 && ny < col) {
                        //
                        //  grid[0][0] == grid[m-1][n-1] == 01
                        if (nx == row - 1 && ny == col - 1) {
                            return step;
                        }
                        currK = grid[nx][ny] == 0 ? currK : --currK;
                        if (currK >= 0) {
                            if (visited[nx][ny] == -1 || (visited[nx][ny] != -1 && currK > visited[nx][ny]) ) {
                                queue.offerLast(new int[]{nx, ny, currK});
                                visited[nx][ny] = currK;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Bidirectional BFS from both ends reduces complexity from O(b^d) to O(b^(d/2)).

---

#### #1319. Number of Operations to Make Network Connected

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-operations-to-make-network-connected/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-operations-to-make-network-connected/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Find the minimum number of additional connections to make a network fully connected.

**Algorithm:**
1. Init Union-Find: parent[i] = i for all nodes.
2. For each edge/pair: union(u, v) to merge components.
3. find(x) with path compression returns root of x's component.
4. Check connectivity or count components using distinct roots.
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    List<Integer>[] edges;
    boolean[] used;
    public int makeConnected(int n, int[][] connections) {
        if (connections.length < n - 1) {
            return -1;
        }
        edges = new List[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            edges[i] = new ArrayList<>();
        }
        for (int[] conn : connections) {
            edges[conn[0]].add(conn[1]);
            edges[conn[1]].add(conn[0]);
        }
        used = new boolean[n];
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (!used[i]) {
                dfs(i);
                ++result;
            }
        }
        return result - 1;
    }
    public void dfs(int u) {
        used[u] = true;
        for (int v : edges[u]) {
            if (!used[v]) {
                dfs(v);
            }
        }
    }
}
class Solution {
    class UnionFind {
        int[] parent;
        int[] size;
        int n;
        int setCount;
        UnionFind(int n) {
            parent = new int[n];
            for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                parent[i] = i;
            }
            size = new int[n];
            Arrays.fill(size, 1);
            this.n = n;
            setCount = n;
        }
        public int find(int x) {
            while(x != parent[x]) {
                x = parent[x];
            }
            return x;
        }
        public boolean union(int x, int y) {
            x = find(x);
            y = find(y);
            if(x == y) {
                return false;
            }
            if(size[x] < size[y]) {
                int temp = x;
                x = y;
                y = temp;
            }
            size[x] += size[y];
            parent[y] = x;
            setCount--;
            return true;
        }
        public boolean same(int x, int y) {
            return find(x) == find(y);
        }
    }
    public int makeConnected(int n, int[][] connections) {
        if (connections.length < n - 1) {
            return -1;
        }
        UnionFind unionFind = new UnionFind(n);
        for(int[] connection : connections) {
            unionFind.union(connection[0], connection[1]);
        }
        return unionFind.setCount - 1;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1559. Detect Cycles in 2D Grid

[leetcode.com/problems/detect-cycles-in-2d-grid/](https://leetcode.com/problems/detect-cycles-in-2d-grid/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Detect if a 2D grid contains a cycle of the same character.

**Algorithm:**
1. Init Union-Find: parent[i] = i for all nodes.
2. For each edge/pair: union(u, v) to merge components.
3. find(x) with path compression returns root of x's component.
4. Check connectivity or count components using distinct roots.
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
// DFS visiting, visited
class Solution {
    public boolean containsCycle(char[][] grid) {
        if(grid == null || grid.length == 0 || grid[0].length == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int rows = grid.length, columns = grid[0].length;
        UnionFind unionFind = new UnionFind(rows * columns);
        for(int i = 0; i < rows; i++) {
            for(int j = 0; j < columns; j++) {
                if(i > 0 && grid[i][j] == grid[i-1][j]) {
                    if(unionFind.same(i * columns + j, (i-1) * columns + j)) { // already in the same component
                        return true;
                    }
                    unionFind.union(i * columns + j, (i-1) * columns + j);
                }
                if(j > 0 && grid[i][j] == grid[i][j-1]) {
                    if(unionFind.same(i * columns + j, i * columns + j - 1)) { // already in the same component
                        return true;
                    }
                    unionFind.union(i * columns + j, i * columns + j - 1);
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    class UnionFind {
        int[] parent;
        int[] size;
        int n;
        int setCount;
        public UnionFind(int n) {
            parent = new int[n];
            for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                parent[i] = i;
            }
            size = new int[n];
            Arrays.fill(size, 1);
            this.n = n;
            setCount = n;
        }
        public int find(int x) {
            return parent[x] == x ? x : find(parent[x]);
        }
        public void union(int x, int y) {
            x = find(x);
            y = find(y);
            if(x == y) return;
            if(size[x] < size[y]) {
                int temp = x;
                x = y;
                y = temp;
            }
            parent[y] = x;
            size[x] += size[y];
            setCount--;
        }
        public boolean same(int x, int y) {
            return find(x) == find(y);
        }
    }
}
class Solution {
    int[][] dirs = {{0, 1}, {0, -1}, {1, 0}, {-1, 0}};
    int m, n;
    boolean[][] visit;
    public boolean containsCycle(char[][] grid) {
        m = grid.length; n = grid[0].length;
        visit = new boolean[m][n];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
                if (!visit[i][j]) {
                    if (dfs(i, j, -1, -1, grid)) return true;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    boolean dfs(int x, int y, int px, int py, char[][] grid) { //xydxdy
        if (visit[x][y]) return true;
        visit[x][y] = true;
        for (int[] dir : dirs) {
            int dx = x + dir[0], dy = y + dir[1];
            if (dx >= 0 && dx < m && dy >= 0 && dy < n && (dx != px || dy != py) && grid[dx][dy] == grid[x][y] && dfs(dx, dy, x, y, grid)) return true;
        }
        return false;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #1631. Path With Minimum Effort

[leetcode.com/problems/path-with-minimum-effort/](https://leetcode.com/problems/path-with-minimum-effort/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the path from top-left to bottom-right minimizing the maximum absolute difference.

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution
{
    //
    //
    private int Rows = 0;
    //
    private int Cols = 0;
    //
    int[][] dir = { { 0, 1 }, { 0, -1 }, { 1, 0 }, { -1, 0 } };
    boolean[][] vis = null;
    private void clearVisRecord()
    {
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                vis[r][c] = false;
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS
    // <r,c>
    private boolean bfs(int[][] heights, int maxValue, int r, int c)
    {
        //
        Queue<int[]> Q = new LinkedList<>();
        //
        Q.offer(new int[] { r, c });
        vis[r][c] = true;
        while (!Q.isEmpty()) {
            //
            int[] topNodePos = Q.poll();
            r = topNodePos[0];
            c = topNodePos[1];
            if (r == (Rows - 1) && c == (Cols - 1)) {
                return true;
            }
            //
            for (int d = 0; d < 4; d++) {
                int nr = r + dir[d][0];
                int nc = c + dir[d][1];
                // <nr, nc>
                if (!(nr < 0 || nc < 0 || nr >= Rows || nc >= Cols)) {
                    //
                    //
                    final int edgeWeight = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[nr][nc]);
                    //
                    if (edgeWeight <= maxValue && !vis[nr][nc]) {
                        Q.offer(new int[] { nr, nc });
                        vis[nr][nc] = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return vis[Rows - 1][Cols - 1];
    }
    // f(x)
    //
    // midValue
    //
    //  <= midValue.
    //
    //  <= midValue
    // -> 0
    // -1
    private int getC(int[][] heights, int midValue)
    {
        clearVisRecord();
        return bfs(heights, midValue, 0, 0) ? 0 : -1;
    }
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights)
    {
        if (heights == null || heights[0] == null) {
            return 0;
        }
        Rows = heights.length;
        Cols = heights[0].length;
        // if just one node
        if (Rows == 1 && Cols == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        // vis
        vis = new boolean[Rows][Cols];
        //
        // /
        int minCost = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int maxCost = 0;
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                //
                if (c + 1 < Cols) {
                    int rightValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r][c + 1]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, rightValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, rightValue);
                }
                if (r + 1 < Rows) {
                    int downValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r + 1][c]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, downValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, downValue);
                }
            }
        }
        //  target
        //   x
        //  x >= target
        //
        // [minCost, maxCost + 1)
        // -1:
        //        0:
        // C[-1,-1,-1,-1, 0, 0, 0, 0]
        //
        //
        // 0
        int l = minCost, r = maxCost + 1;
        while (l < r) {
            final int mid = l + ((r - l) >> 1);
            final int mv = getC(heights, mid);
            if (mv < 0) {
                l = mid + 1;
            } else {
                r = mid;
            }
        }
        return l;
    }
}
class UnionFind
{
    private int[] F = null;
    public UnionFind(int n) { Init(n); }
    private void Init(int n)
    {
        F = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            F[i] = i;
        }
    }
    public int Find(int x)
    {
        if (x == F[x]) {
            return x;
        }
        F[x] = Find(F[x]);
        return F[x];
    }
    public void Union(int x, int y) { F[Find(x)] = Find(y); }
}
class Solution
{
    //
    //
    private int Rows = 0;
    //
    private int Cols = 0;
    private int getPointMapping(int r, int c) { return r * Cols + c; }
    // f(x)
    //
    // midValue
    //
    //  <= midValue.
    //
    //  <= midValue
    // -> 0
    // -1
    private int getC(int[][] heights, int midValue)
    {
        UnionFind uf = new UnionFind(Rows * Cols);
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                //
                if (c + 1 < Cols) {
                    int edgeCost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r][c + 1]);
                    if (edgeCost <= midValue) {
                        uf.Union(getPointMapping(r, c), getPointMapping(r, c + 1));
                    }
                }
                if (r + 1 < Rows) {
                    int edgeCost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r + 1][c]);
                    if (edgeCost <= midValue) {
                        uf.Union(getPointMapping(r, c), getPointMapping(r + 1, c));
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return uf.Find(getPointMapping(0, 0)) ==
        uf.Find(getPointMapping(Rows-1, Cols-1)) ? 0 : -1;
    }
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights)
    {
        if (heights == null || heights[0] == null) {
            return 0;
        }
        Rows = heights.length;
        Cols = heights[0].length;
        // if just one node
        if (Rows == 1 && Cols == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        // /
        int minCost = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int maxCost = 0;
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                //
                if (c + 1 < Cols) {
                    int rightValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r][c + 1]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, rightValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, rightValue);
                }
                if (r + 1 < Rows) {
                    int downValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r + 1][c]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, downValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, downValue);
                }
            }
        }
        //  target
        //   x
        //  x >= target
        //
        // [minCost, maxCost + 1)
        // -1:
        //        0:
        // C[-1,-1,-1,-1, 0, 0, 0, 0]
        //
        //
        // 0
        int l = minCost, r = maxCost + 1;
        while (l < r) {
            final int mid = l + ((r - l) >> 1);
            final int mv = getC(heights, mid);
            if (mv < 0) {
                l = mid + 1;
            } else {
                r = mid;
            }
        }
        return l;
    }
}
class Solution
{
    //
    //
    private int Rows = 0;
    //
    private int Cols = 0;
    //
    int[][] dir = { { 0, 1 }, { 0, -1 }, { 1, 0 }, { -1, 0 } };
    boolean[][] vis = null;
    private void clearVisRecord()
    {
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                vis[r][c] = false;
            }
        }
    }
    // DFS
    // <r,c>
    private boolean dfs(int[][] heights, int maxValue, int r, int c)
    {
        // <rows-1, cols-1>
        if (r == Rows - 1 && c == Cols - 1) {
            return true;
        }
        //  <r,c>
        for (int d = 0; d < 4; d++) {
            final int nr = r + dir[d][0];
            final int nc = c + dir[d][1];
            //
            if ((!(nr < 0 || nc < 0 || nr >= Rows || nc >= Cols)) && !vis[nr][nc]) {
                //
                final int cost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[nr][nc]);
                // midValue
                if (cost <= maxValue) {
                    vis[nr][nc] = true;
                    if (dfs(heights, maxValue, nr, nc)) {
                        return true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    // f(x)
    //
    // midValue
    //
    //  <= midValue.
    //
    //  <= midValue
    // -> 0
    // -1
    private int getC(int[][] heights, int midValue)
    {
        clearVisRecord();
        vis[0][0] = true;
        return dfs(heights, midValue, 0, 0) ? 0 : -1;
    }
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights)
    {
        if (heights == null || heights[0] == null) {
            return 0;
        }
        Rows = heights.length;
        Cols = heights[0].length;
        // if just one node
        if (Rows == 1 && Cols == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        // vis
        vis = new boolean[Rows][Cols];
        //
        // /
        int minCost = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int maxCost = 0;
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                //
                if (c + 1 < Cols) {
                    int rightValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r][c + 1]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, rightValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, rightValue);
                }
                if (r + 1 < Rows) {
                    int downValue = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r + 1][c]);
                    minCost = Math.min(minCost, downValue);
                    maxCost = Math.max(maxCost, downValue);
                }
            }
        }
        //  target
        //   x
        //  x >= target
        //
        // [minCost, maxCost + 1)
        // -1:
        //        0:
        // C[-1,-1,-1,-1, 0, 0, 0, 0]
        //
        //
        // 0
        int l = minCost, r = maxCost + 1;
        while (l < r) {
            final int mid = l + ((r - l) >> 1);
            final int mv = getC(heights, mid);
            if (mv < 0) {
                l = mid + 1;
            } else {
                r = mid;
            }
        }
        return l;
    }
}
class Solution
{
    //
    private int Rows = 0;
    //
    private int Cols = 0;
    //
    int[][] dir = { { 0, 1 }, { 0, -1 }, { 1, 0 }, { -1, 0 } };
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights)
    {
        if (heights == null || heights[0] == null) {
            return 0;
        }
        Rows = heights.length;
        Cols = heights[0].length;
        //
        if (Rows == 1 && Cols == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        // BF
        // Rows + Cols
        // Rows + Cols
        //
        //
        final int maxDist = Integer.MAX_VALUE >> 4;
        int[][] dist = new int[Rows][Cols];
        //
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                dist[r][c] = maxDist;
            }
        }
        dist[0][0] = 0;
        final int maxUpdateTimes = Rows + Cols;
        // BF
        for (int updateTimes = 0; updateTimes < maxUpdateTimes; updateTimes++) {
            boolean hasUpdateItem = false;
            //
            for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
                for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                    for (int d = 0; d < 4; d++) {
                        int nr = r + dir[d][0];
                        int nc = c + dir[d][1];
                        if (!(nr < 0 || nc < 0 || nr >= Rows || nc >= Cols)) {
                            //
                            final int cost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[nr][nc]);
                            //
                            final int nextCost = Math.max(dist[r][c], cost);
                            if (nextCost < dist[nr][nc]) {
                                dist[nr][nc] = nextCost;
                                hasUpdateItem = true;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            //
            if (!hasUpdateItem) {
                break;
            }
        }
        return dist[Rows - 1][Cols - 1];
    }
}
class UnionFind
{
    private int[] F = null;
    public UnionFind(int n) { Init(n); }
    private void Init(int n)
    {
        F = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            F[i] = i;
        }
    }
    public int Find(int x)
    {
        if (x == F[x]) {
            return x;
        }
        F[x] = Find(F[x]);
        return F[x];
    }
    public void Union(int x, int y) { F[Find(x)] = Find(y); }
}
class Solution
{
    //
    private int Rows = 0;
    //
    private int Cols = 0;
    //
    private int[][] dir = { { 0, 1 }, { 0, -1 }, { 1, 0 }, { -1, 0 } };
    private int getPointMapping(int r, int c) { return r * Cols + c; }
    private void
    putEdge(int[][] edges, int iter, int r, int c, int nr, int nc, int cost)
    {
        edges[iter][0] = getPointMapping(r, c);
        edges[iter][1] = getPointMapping(nr, nc);
        edges[iter][2] = cost;
    }
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights)
    {
        if (heights == null || heights[0] == null) {
            return 0;
        }
        Rows = heights.length;
        Cols = heights[0].length;
        //
        if (Rows == 1 && Cols == 1) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        //
        final int hNumber = Rows * (Cols - 1);
        //
        final int vNumber = Cols * (Rows - 1);
        //
        //
        int[][] edges = new int[hNumber + vNumber][3];
        //
        int edgeIter = 0;
        for (int r = 0; r < Rows; r++) {
            for (int c = 0; c < Cols; c++) {
                //
                if (c + 1 < Cols) {
                    int edgeCost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r][c + 1]);
                    putEdge(edges, edgeIter, r, c, r, c + 1, edgeCost);
                    edgeIter++;
                }
                if (r + 1 < Rows) {
                    int edgeCost = Math.abs(heights[r][c] - heights[r + 1][c]);
                    putEdge(edges, edgeIter, r, c, r + 1, c, edgeCost);
                    edgeIter++;
                }
            }
        }
        //
        Arrays.sort(edges, (a, b) -> a[2] - b[2]);
        //
        final int totalNodes = Rows * Cols;
        UnionFind uf = new UnionFind(totalNodes);
        final int src = 0;
        final int dst = getPointMapping(Rows - 1, Cols - 1);
        for (int[] edge : edges) {
            uf.Union(edge[0], edge[1]);
            //  src dst
            // cost
            if (uf.Find(src) == uf.Find(dst)) {
                return edge[2];
            }
        }
        assert 0 > -1; // Should not reach here!
        return 0;
    }
}
class Solution {
    private static final int[][] DIRS = new int[][]{{-1, 0}, {1, 0}, {0, -1}, {0, 1}};
    public int minimumEffortPath(int[][] heights) {
        int row = heights.length, col = heights[0].length;
        int[][] f = new int[row][col];
        // d[i][j]: [0, 0][i, j]  maximum absolute difference in heights
        // minHeapbellmanFord
        for(int i = 0; i < row; i++) {
            for(int j = 0; j < col; j++) {
                f[i][j] = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
            }
        }
        f[0][0] = 0;
        // min queue
        PriorityQueue<int[]> queue = new PriorityQueue<>((a, b) -> (f[a[0]][a[1]] - f[b[0]][b[1]]));
        queue.offer(new int[]{0, 0});
        while(!queue.isEmpty()) {
            int[] current = queue.poll();
            // //
            // if(current[0] == row-1 && current[1] == col - 1) {
                //     return f[row-1][col-1];
                // }
            for(int[] dir : DIRS) {
                int x = current[0] + dir[0];
                int y = current[1] + dir[1];
                if(x >= 0 && x < row && y >= 0 && y < col) {
                    int height = Math.abs(heights[current[0]][current[1]] - heights[x][y]);
                    int toUpdate = Math.max(f[current[0]][current[1]], height);
                    // f[i][j]
                    // update
                    if (toUpdate < f[x][y]) {
                        f[x][y] = toUpdate;
                        queue.offer(new int[]{x, y});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return f[row-1][col-1];
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Bidirectional BFS from both ends reduces complexity from O(b^d) to O(b^(d/2)).
- After sorting, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of linear scan for lookups.

---

#### #1743. Restore the Array From Adjacent Pairs

[leetcode.com/problems/restore-the-array-from-adjacent-pairs/](https://leetcode.com/problems/restore-the-array-from-adjacent-pairs/) · `O(V+E) / O(V)`

**Q:** Restore the original array from a list of all adjacent pairs.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build adjacency list from input edges.
2. DFS from each unvisited node; mark visited before recursing.
3. Detect cycles: node in current DFS path → cycle found.
4. Collect result (component count, topological order, etc.).
`O(V+E) / O(V)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    //
    public int[] restoreArray(int[][] adjacentPairs) {
        Map<Integer, List<Integer>> map = new HashMap<>();
        for (int[] adjacentPair : adjacentPairs) {
            map.computeIfAbsent(adjacentPair[0], list -> new ArrayList<>()).add(adjacentPair[1]);
            map.computeIfAbsent(adjacentPair[1], list -> new ArrayList<>()).add(adjacentPair[0]);
        }
        int n = adjacentPairs.length + 1;
        int[] ret = new int[n];
        for (Map.Entry<Integer, List<Integer>> entry : map.entrySet()) {
            int e = entry.getKey();
            List<Integer> adj = entry.getValue();
            if (adj.size() == 1) {
                ret[0] = e;
                break;
            }
        }
        ret[1] = map.get(ret[0]).get(0);
        for (int i = 2; i < n; i++) {
            List<Integer> adj = map.get(ret[i - 1]);
            ret[i] = ret[i - 2] == adj.get(0) ? adj.get(1) : adj.get(0);
        }
        return ret;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

### String (87 problems)

#### #214. Shortest Palindrome

[leetcode.com/problems/shortest-palindrome/](https://leetcode.com/problems/shortest-palindrome/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the shortest palindrome by adding characters in front of a string.

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String shortestPalindrome(String s) {
        String ss = s + '#' + new StringBuilder(s).reverse();
        int max = getLastNext(ss);
        return new StringBuilder(s.substring(max)).reverse() + s;
    }
    // next
    public int getLastNext(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        char[] c = s.toCharArray();
        int[] next = new int[n + 1];
        next[0] = -1;
        next[1] = 0;
        int k = 0;
        int i = 2;
        while (i <= n) {
            if (k == -1 || c[i - 1] == c[k]) {
                next[i] = k + 1;
                k++;
                i++;
            } else {
                k = next[k];
            }
        }
        return next[n];
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #6. ZigZag Conversion

[leetcode.com/problems/zigzag-conversion/](https://leetcode.com/problems/zigzag-conversion/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Convert a string into a zigzag pattern across numRows rows and read it row by row.

**Example:** `s = "PAYPALISHIRING", numRows = 3` → `"PAHNAPLSIIGYIR"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use sliding window with HashMap to track character frequencies.
2. Expand right; when window invalid shrink from left.
3. Track result (max window, min window, count of valid windows).
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String convert(String s, int numRows) {
        if (numRows == 1) return s;
        List<StringBuilder> rows = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < Math.min(numRows, s.length()); i++) {
            rows.add(new StringBuilder());
        }
        int curRow = 0;
        boolean goingDown = false;
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            rows.get(curRow).append(c);
            if (curRow == 0 || curRow == numRows - 1) {
                goingDown = !goingDown;
            }
            curRow += goingDown ? 1 : -1;
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        for (StringBuilder row : rows) {
            builder.append(row);
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #7. Reverse Integer

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-integer/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-integer/) · `O(log x) / O(1)`

**Q:** Reverse the digits of a 32-bit signed integer. Return 0 if the reversed value overflows.

**Example:** `x = 123` → `321`

**Algorithm:**
1. Check for INT_MIN edge case.
2. Extract last digit with x % 10; build result = result * 10 + digit.
3. Before each multiply, check overflow: result > INT_MAX/10.
4. Advance x = x / 10 until 0.
`O(log x) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int reverse(int x) {
        int result = 0;
        while(x != 0) {
            if(result < Integer.MIN_VALUE / 10 || result > Integer.MAX_VALUE / 10) return 0;
            int digit = x % 10;
            x = x / 10;
            result = result * 10 + digit;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Handle Integer.MIN_VALUE specially before reversing to avoid overflow in abs().

---

#### #8. String to Integer (atoi)

[leetcode.com/problems/string-to-integer-atoi/](https://leetcode.com/problems/string-to-integer-atoi/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Implement atoi: parse a string, skip whitespace, handle optional sign, read digits, clamp to 32-bit integer range.

**Example:** `s = "42"` → `42`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
public class Solution {
    public int myAtoi(String str) {
        int len = str.length();
        // str.charAt(i)
        char[] charArray = str.toCharArray();
        // 1
        int index = 0;
        while (index < len && charArray[index] == ' ') {
            index++;
        }
        // 2 "      "
        if (index == len) {
            return 0;
        }
        // 3 1
        int sign = 1;
        char firstChar = charArray[index];
        if (firstChar == '+') {
            index++;
        } else if (firstChar == '-') {
            index++;
            sign = -1;
        }
        // 4
        //  long
        int result = 0;
        while (index < len) {
            char currChar = charArray[index];
            // 4.1
            if (currChar > '9' || currChar < '0') {
                break;
            }
            //  32  10
            if (result > Integer.MAX_VALUE / 10 || (result == Integer.MAX_VALUE / 10 && (currChar - '0') > Integer.MAX_VALUE % 10)) {
                return Integer.MAX_VALUE;
            }
            if (result < Integer.MIN_VALUE / 10 || (result == Integer.MIN_VALUE / 10 && (currChar - '0') > -(Integer.MIN_VALUE % 10))) {
                return Integer.MIN_VALUE;
            }
            // 4.2
            result = result * 10 + sign * (currChar - '0');
            index++;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
public class Solution {
    /**
    * @param s: A string
    * @return: An integer
    */
    public int atoi(String s) {
        char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
        if(arr.length == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int i = 0;
        while(i < arr.length && arr[i] == ' ') {
            i++;
        }
        int sign = 1;
        if(arr[i] == '-') {
            sign = -1;
            i++;
        } else if(arr[i] == '+') {
            i++;
        }
        int result = 0;
        while(i < arr.length) {
            if(arr[i] < '0' || arr[i] > '9') {
                return result;
            }
            int digit = arr[i] - '0';
            if(result > Integer.MAX_VALUE / 10 || result == Integer.MAX_VALUE / 10 && digit > Integer.MAX_VALUE % 10) {
                return Integer.MAX_VALUE;
            }
            if(result < Integer.MIN_VALUE / 10 || result == Integer.MIN_VALUE / 10 && digit >  -(Integer.MIN_VALUE % 10)) {
                return Integer.MIN_VALUE;
            }
            result = result * 10 + sign * digit;
            i++;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #12. Integer to Roman

[leetcode.com/problems/integer-to-roman/](https://leetcode.com/problems/integer-to-roman/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Convert an integer to its Roman numeral representation (values 1–3999).

**Example:** `num = 3` → `"III"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    int[] values = {1000, 900, 500, 400, 100, 90, 50, 40, 10, 9, 5, 4, 1};
    String[] symbols = {"M", "CM", "D", "CD", "C", "XC", "L", "XL", "X", "IX", "V", "IV", "I"};
    public String intToRoman(int num) {
        StringBuilder roman = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < values.length; ++i) {
            int value = values[i];
            String symbol = symbols[i];
            while (num >= value) {
                num -= value;
                roman.append(symbol);
            }
            if (num == 0) {
                break;
            }
        }
        return roman.toString();
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #13. Roman to Integer

[leetcode.com/problems/roman-to-integer/](https://leetcode.com/problems/roman-to-integer/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Convert a Roman numeral string to an integer.

**Example:** `s = "III"` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use sliding window with HashMap to track character frequencies.
2. Expand right; when window invalid shrink from left.
3. Track result (max window, min window, count of valid windows).
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    Map<Character, Integer> symbolValues = new HashMap<>() {{
            put('I', 1);
            put('V', 5);
            put('X', 10);
            put('L', 50);
            put('C', 100);
            put('D', 500);
            put('M', 1000);
        }};
    public int romanToInt(String s) {
        int result = 0;
        int n = s.length();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            int value = symbolValues.get(s.charAt(i));
            if (i + 1< n && value < symbolValues.get(s.charAt(i + 1))) {
                result -= value;
            } else {
                result += value;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #14. Longest Common Prefix

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-common-prefix/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-common-prefix/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the longest common prefix string among an array of strings. Return '' if none.

**Example:** `strs = ["flower","flow","flight"]` → `"fl"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String longestCommonPrefix(String[] strs) {
        if(strs == null || strs.length <= 1) {
            return strs[0];
        }
        return longestCommonPrefix(strs, 0, strs.length-1);
    }
    public String longestCommonPrefix(String[] strs, int start, int end) {
        if(start == end) {
            return strs[start];
        }
        int mid = start + (end - start) / 2;
        String left = longestCommonPrefix(strs, start, mid);
        String right = longestCommonPrefix(strs, mid+1, end);
        return longestCommonPrefix(left, right);
    }
    public String longestCommonPrefix(String left, String right) {
        for(int i = 0; i < left.length(); i++) {
            if(i >= right.length() || left.charAt(i) != right.charAt(i)) {
                return left.substring(0, i);
            }
        }
        return left;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #28. Implement strStr()

[leetcode.com/problems/implement-strstr/](https://leetcode.com/problems/implement-strstr/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the first occurrence of needle in haystack. Return -1 if not found.

**Example:** `haystack = "sadbutsad", needle = "sad"` → `0`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    private int[] buildNext(String sub) {
        final int N = sub == null ? 0 : sub.length();
        int[] next = new int[N + 1];
        int i = 0;
        int j = -1;
        next[0] = -1;
        while (i < N) {
            if (j == -1 || sub.charAt(i) == sub.charAt(j)) {
                i++;
                j++;
                next[i] = j;
            } else {
                j = next[j];
            }
        }
        return next;
    }
    // return the first occurrence of sub in main, or -1 if not exist
    public int KMP(String main, String sub) {
        int i = 0;
        int j = 0;
        final int alen = main.length();
        final int blen = sub.length();
        int[] next = buildNext(sub);
        while (i < alen && j < blen) {
            if (j == -1 || main.charAt(i) == sub.charAt(j)) {
                //
                //
                i++;
                j++;
            } else {
                j = next[j];
            }
        }
        //
        return j == blen ? i - blen : -1;
    }
    public int strStr(String main, String sub) {
        return KMP(main, sub);
    }
}
class Solution {
    class RabinKarpHashing {
        long[] h, p;
        int P = 27;
        RabinKarpHashing(String s) {
            int n = s.length();
            h = new long[n + 1]; p = new long[n + 1];
            p[0] = 1;
            for (int i = 1; i <= s.length(); i++) {
                h[i] = h[i - 1] * P + s.charAt(i - 1);
                p[i] = p[i - 1] * P;
            }
        }
        private long getHashKey(int i, int j) {
            // [i, j]
            return h[j] - h[i - 1] * p[j - i + 1];
        }
    }
    public int strStr(String haystack, String needle) {
        if(haystack.length() < needle.length()) {
            return -1;
        }
        RabinKarpHashing haystackHash = new RabinKarpHashing(haystack);
        RabinKarpHashing needleHash = new RabinKarpHashing(needle);
        for(int i = 1; i + needle.length() - 1 <= haystack.length(); i++) {
            if(haystackHash.getHashKey(i, i + needle.length() - 1) == needleHash.getHashKey(1, needle.length())) {
                return i - 1;
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #38. Count and Say

[leetcode.com/problems/count-and-say/](https://leetcode.com/problems/count-and-say/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Generate the nth term of the 'count and say' sequence: read digits of previous term aloud.

**Example:** `n = 4` → `"1211"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String countAndSay(int n) {
        String str = "1";
        for (int i = 2; i <= n; ++i) {
            StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
            int start = 0;
            int pos = 0;
            while (pos < str.length()) {
                while (pos < str.length() && str.charAt(pos) == str.charAt(start)) {
                    pos++;
                }
                builder.append(Integer.toString(pos - start)).append(str.charAt(start));
                start = pos;
            }
            str = builder.toString();
        }
        return str;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #43. Multiply Strings

[leetcode.com/problems/multiply-strings/](https://leetcode.com/problems/multiply-strings/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Multiply two non-negative integers given as strings. Return product as a string.

**Example:** `num1 = "2", num2 = "3"` → `"6"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String multiply(String num1, String num2) {
        if (num1.equals("0") || num2.equals("0")) {
            return "0";
        }
        int[] result = new int[num1.length() + num2.length()];
        for (int i = num1.length() - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            int n1 = num1.charAt(i) - '0';
            for (int j = num2.length() - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                int n2 = num2.charAt(j) - '0';
                int sum = (result[i + j + 1] + n1 * n2);
                result[i + j + 1] = sum % 10;
                result[i + j] += sum / 10;
            }
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
            if (i == 0 && result[i] == 0) continue; // leading zeros
            builder.append(result[i]);
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #49. Group Anagrams

[leetcode.com/problems/group-anagrams/](https://leetcode.com/problems/group-anagrams/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Group an array of strings so that anagrams are together.

**Example:** `strs = ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]` → `[["bat"],["nat","tan"],["ate","eat","tea"]]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use int[26] frequency array for O(1) char counting (lowercase letters).
2. Scan string, increment/decrement counts.
3. Check condition on frequency array (all zeros, single non-zero, etc.).
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<List<String>> groupAnagrams(String[] strs) {
        Map<String, List<String>> map = new HashMap<>();
        for(String s : strs) {
            String hashKey = generateHashCode(s);
            map.computeIfAbsent(hashKey, list -> new ArrayList<>()).add(s);
        }
        return new ArrayList<>(map.values());
    }
    public String generateHashCode(String s) {
        int[] count = new int[26];
        for(char ch : s.toCharArray()) {
            count[ch - 'a']++;
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        for(int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            builder.append((char) ('a'+ i));
            builder.append(count[i]);
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- Sorting each string as key: O(n * k log k). Alternative: use char-frequency tuple as key for O(n*k).

#### #242. Valid Anagram

[leetcode.com/problems/valid-anagram/](https://leetcode.com/problems/valid-anagram/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Determine if two strings are anagrams of each other.

**Example:** `s = "anagram", t = "nagaram"` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Create int[26] for s and int[26] for t.
2. Count character frequencies.
3. Return Arrays.equals(sCount, tCount).
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isAnagram(String s, String t) {
        return generateHashCode(s).equals(generateHashCode(t));
    }
    public String generateHashCode(String s) {
        int[] count = new int[26];
        for(char ch : s.toCharArray()) {
            count[ch - 'a']++;
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        for(int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            builder.append((char) (i + 'a') + count[i] + "");
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- int[26] array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-letter-only inputs.

#### #58. Length of Last Word

[leetcode.com/problems/length-of-last-word/](https://leetcode.com/problems/length-of-last-word/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Return the length of the last word in a string (word = max sequence of non-space chars).

**Example:** `s = "Hello World"` → `5`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int lengthOfLastWord(String s) {
        int index = s.length() - 1;
        while (s.charAt(index) == ' ') {
            index--;
        }
        int wordLength = 0;
        while (index >= 0 && s.charAt(index) != ' ') {
            wordLength++;
            index--;
        }
        return wordLength;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #65. Valid Number

[leetcode.com/problems/valid-number/](https://leetcode.com/problems/valid-number/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Validate whether a string is a valid decimal number (integers, fractions, exponents).

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isNumber(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        char[] cs = s.toCharArray();
        int index = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (cs[i] == 'e' || cs[i] == 'E') {
                if (index == -1) index = i;
                else return false;
            }
        }
        boolean result = true;
        if (index != -1) {
            result &= check(cs, 0, index - 1, false);
            result &= check(cs, index + 1, n - 1, true);
        } else {
            result &= check(cs, 0, n - 1, false);
        }
        return result;
    }
    boolean check(char[] cs, int start, int end, boolean mustInteger) {
        if (start > end) return false;
        if (cs[start] == '+' || cs[start] == '-') start++;
        boolean hasDot = false, hasNum = false;
        for (int i = start; i <= end; i++) {
            if (cs[i] == '.') {
                if (mustInteger || hasDot) return false;
                hasDot = true;
            } else if (cs[i] >= '0' && cs[i] <= '9') {
                hasNum = true;
            } else {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return hasNum;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #67. Add Binary

[leetcode.com/problems/add-binary/](https://leetcode.com/problems/add-binary/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Add two binary strings and return their binary sum as a string.

**Example:** `a = "11", b = "1"` → `"100"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String addBinary(String a, String b) {
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        int i = a.length() - 1 , j = b.length() - 1;
        int carry = 0;
        while(i >= 0 || j >= 0 || carry != 0) {
            int sum = 0;
            if(i >= 0) {
                sum += a.charAt(i) - '0';
                i--;
            }
            if(j >= 0) {
                sum += b.charAt(j) - '0';
                j--;
            }
            sum += carry;
            if(sum == 0 || sum == 1) {
                carry = 0;
            } else {
                sum = sum - 2;
                carry = 1;
            }
            sb.append(sum + "");
        }
        return sb.reverse().toString();
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #68. Text Justification

[leetcode.com/problems/text-justification/](https://leetcode.com/problems/text-justification/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Format words into lines of maxWidth, fully justified (equal spaces, last line left-aligned).

**Algorithm:**
1. Init left=0, windowSum=0, result.
2. Expand right: add nums[right] to window.
3. If window exceeds size k: remove nums[left++].
4. Update result when window reaches target size.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<String> fullJustify(String[] words, int maxWidth) {
        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
        int right = 0, n = words.length;
        while (true) {
            int left = right; //  words
            int sumLen = 0; //
            //
            while (right < n && sumLen + words[right].length() + right - left <= maxWidth) {
                sumLen += words[right++].length();
            }
            //
            if (right == n) {
                StringBuffer sb = join(words, left, n, " ");
                sb.append(blank(maxWidth - sb.length()));
                result.add(sb.toString());
                return result;
            }
            int numWords = right - left;
            int numSpaces = maxWidth - sumLen;
            //
            if (numWords == 1) {
                StringBuffer sb = new StringBuffer(words[left]);
                sb.append(blank(numSpaces));
                result.add(sb.toString());
                continue;
            }
            //
            int avgSpaces = numSpaces / (numWords - 1);
            int extraSpaces = numSpaces % (numWords - 1);
            StringBuffer sb = new StringBuffer();
            sb.append(join(words, left, left + extraSpaces + 1, blank(avgSpaces + 1))); //
            sb.append(blank(avgSpaces));
            sb.append(join(words, left + extraSpaces + 1, right, blank(avgSpaces))); //
            result.add(sb.toString());
        }
    }
    // blank  n
    public String blank(int n) {
        StringBuffer sb = new StringBuffer();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            sb.append(' ');
        }
        return sb.toString();
    }
    // join  sep  [left, right)  words
    public StringBuffer join(String[] words, int left, int right, String sep) {
        StringBuffer sb = new StringBuffer(words[left]);
        for (int i = left + 1; i < right; ++i) {
            sb.append(sep);
            sb.append(words[i]);
        }
        return sb;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #161. One Edit Distance

[leetcode.com/problems/one-edit-distance/](https://leetcode.com/problems/one-edit-distance/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Determine if two strings are one edit distance apart (insert, delete, or replace one char).

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isOneEditDistance(String s, String t) {
        if(s == null || t == null) {
            return false;
        }
        int n = s.length(), m = t.length();
        if(Math.abs(n - m) > 1) {
            return false;
        }
        int i = 0;
        while(i < Math.min(n, m) && s.charAt(i) == t.charAt(i)) {
            i++;
        }
        boolean result = false;
        if(i + 1 <= m) {
            result |= s.substring(i).equals(t.substring(i+1));
        }
        if(i + 1 <= n) {
            result |= s.substring(i+1).equals(t.substring(i));
        }
        if(i + 1 <= m && i + 1 <= n) {
            result |= s.substring(i+1).equals(t.substring(i+1));
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #125. Valid Palindrome

[leetcode.com/problems/valid-palindrome/](https://leetcode.com/problems/valid-palindrome/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Check if a string is a palindrome, ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case.

**Example:** `s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify pointer strategy: opposite ends or same direction.
2. Move pointers based on comparison with target condition.
3. Track result/count when condition satisfied.
4. Handle duplicates by skipping equal adjacent elements.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isPalindrome(String s) {
        if(s == null || s.length() == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        char[] arr = s.toUpperCase().toCharArray();
        int left = 0, right = s.length() - 1;
        while(left < right) {
            while(left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(arr[left])) {
                left++;
            }
            while(left < right && !Character.isLetterOrDigit(arr[right])) {
                right--;
            }
            if(arr[left] != arr[right]) {
                return false;
            }
            left++;
            right--;
        }
        return true;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public boolean isPalindrome(String s) {
        char[] arr = s.toLowerCase().toCharArray();
        int end = removeSpace(arr);
        return isPalindrome(arr, 0, end - 1);
    }
    public int removeSpace(char[] arr) {
        int i = 0; // [0, i)
        for(int j = 0 ; j < arr.length; j++) {
            if(Character.isLetterOrDigit(arr[j])) {
                arr[i] = arr[j];
                i++;
            }
        }
        return i;
    }
    public boolean isPalindrome(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        while(i < j) {
            if(arr[i++] != arr[j--]) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #680. Valid Palindrome II

[leetcode.com/problems/valid-palindrome-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/valid-palindrome-ii/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Check if a string is a palindrome after deleting at most one character.

**Example:** `s = "aba"` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
        if(s == null || s.length() == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
        for(int i = 0, j = arr.length - 1; i < j; i++, j--) {
            if(arr[i] != arr[j]) {
                return validPalindrome(arr, i + 1, j) || validPalindrome(arr, i, j - 1);
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
    public boolean validPalindrome(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        for(; i < j; i++, j--) {
            if(arr[i] != arr[j]) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
        if(s == null || s.length() == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
        for(int i = 0, j = arr.length - 1; i < j; i++, j--) {
            if(arr[i] != arr[j]) {
                return validPalindrome(arr, i + 1, j) || validPalindrome(arr, i, j - 1);
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
    public boolean validPalindrome(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        while(i < j) {
            if(arr[i++] != arr[j--]) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #151. Reverse Words in a String

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Reverse the order of words in a string (split on spaces, handle multiple spaces).

**Example:** `s = "the sky is blue"` → `"blue is sky the"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String reverseWords(String s) {
        char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
        reverse(arr, 0, arr.length - 1);
        int i = 0;
        while(i < arr.length) {
            if(arr[i] == ' ') {
                i++;
            } else {
                int j = i;
                while(j + 1 < arr.length && arr[j+1] != ' ') {
                    j++;
                }
                reverse(arr, i, j);
                i = j + 1;
            }
        }
        return removeRedundantSpace(arr);
    }
    public void swap(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        char temp = arr[i];
        arr[i] = arr[j];
        arr[j] = temp;
    }
    public void reverse(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        for(; i < j; i++, j--) {
            swap(arr, i, j);
        }
    }
    public String removeRedundantSpace(char[] arr) {
        int i = 0; // [0, i)
        for(int j = 0; j < arr.length; j++) {
            if(arr[j] != ' ') {
                arr[i] = arr[j];
                i++;
            } else if(i > 0 && arr[i-1] != ' ') {
                arr[i] = arr[j];
                i++;
            }
        }
        if(arr[i-1] == ' ') {
            i--;
        }
        return new String(arr, 0, i);
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #186. Reverse Words in a String II

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string-ii/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Reverse words in a character array in-place (words separated by spaces).

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public void reverseWords(char[] arr) {
        int n = arr.length;
        reverse(arr, 0, n - 1);
        int start = 0;
        while(start < n) {
            if(arr[start] == ' ') {
                start++;
                continue;
            }
            int end = start;
            while(end < n && arr[end] != ' ') {
                end++;
            }
            reverse(arr, start, end - 1);
            start = end + 1;
        }
        return ;
    }
    public void swap(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        char temp = arr[i];
        arr[i] = arr[j];
        arr[j] = temp;
    }
    public void reverse(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        for(;i < j; i++, j--) {
            swap(arr, i, j);
        }
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #557. Reverse Words in a String III

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string-iii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-words-in-a-string-iii/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Reverse individual words in a string while preserving word order.

**Example:** `s = "Let us go"` → `"Let su og"... actually "Let us og"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String reverseWords(String s) {
        if(s == null || s.length() == 0) {
            return s;
        }
        char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
        int i = 0;
        while(i < arr.length) {
            int j = i;
            while(j + 1 < arr.length && arr[j+1] != ' ') {
                j++;
            }
            reverse(arr, i, j);
            i = j + 2;
        }
        return new String(arr);
    }
    private void swap(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        char temp = arr[i];
        arr[i] = arr[j];
        arr[j] = temp;
    }
    private void reverse(char[] arr, int i, int j) {
        for(; i < j; i++, j--) {
            swap(arr, i, j);
        }
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #163. Missing Ranges

[leetcode.com/problems/missing-ranges/](https://leetcode.com/problems/missing-ranges/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Given a sorted array, return missing ranges between lower and upper bounds.

**Example:** `nums = [0,1,3,50,75], lower = 0, upper = 99` → `["2","4->49","51->74","76->99"]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<String> findMissingRanges(int[] nums, int lower, int upper) {
        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
        int previous = lower - 1;
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if (nums[i] - previous == 2) {
                result.add(String.valueOf(previous + 1));
            } else if (nums[i] - previous >= 3) {
                result.add((previous + 1) + "->" + (nums[i] - 1));
            }
            previous = nums[i]; // 'int' to 'long'
        }
        if (upper - previous == 1) {
            result.add(String.valueOf(previous + 1));
        } else if (upper - previous >= 2) {
            result.add((previous + 1) + "->" + upper);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

#### #165. Compare Version Numbers

[leetcode.com/problems/compare-version-numbers/](https://leetcode.com/problems/compare-version-numbers/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Compare two version number strings (dot-separated integers). Return -1, 0, or 1.

**Example:** `version1 = "1.01", version2 = "1.001"` → `0`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify string operation: search, transform, validate, or parse.
2. Use appropriate data structure: char array, HashMap, stack, or two pointers.
3. Process characters with relevant conditions.
4. Build or return result.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int compareVersion(String v1, String v2) {
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        int n = v1.length(), m = v2.length();
        while(i < n || j < m)
        {
            int num1 = 0, num2 = 0;
            while(i < n && v1.charAt(i) != '.') {
                num1 = num1 * 10 + v1.charAt(i++) - '0';
            }
            while(j < m && v2.charAt(j) != '.') {
                num2 = num2 * 10 + v2.charAt(j++) - '0';
            }
            if(num1 > num2) {
                return 1;
            } else if(num1 < num2) {
                return -1;
            }
            i++; j++;
        }
        return 0;
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.

---

#### #166. Fraction to Recurring Decimal

[leetcode.com/problems/fraction-to-recurring-decimal/](https://leetcode.com/problems/fraction-to-recurring-decimal/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Convert a fraction numerator/denominator to a decimal string, noting repeating parts in parentheses.

**Algorithm:**
1. Use sliding window with HashMap to track character frequencies.
2. Expand right; when window invalid shrink from left.
3. Track result (max window, min window, count of valid windows).
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String fractionToDecimal(int numerator, int denominator) {
        //  long
        long a = numerator, b = denominator;
        //
        if (a % b == 0) {
            return String.valueOf(a / b);
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        //
        if (a * b < 0) {
            builder.append('-');
        }
        a = Math.abs(a); b = Math.abs(b);
        //  a
        builder.append(String.valueOf(a / b) + ".");
        long remainder = a % b;
        Map<Long, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        while (remainder != 0) {
            //
            map.put(remainder, builder.length());
            remainder *= 10;
            builder.append(remainder / b);
            remainder %= b;
            //  [  ]
            if (map.containsKey(remainder)) {
                int index = map.get(remainder);
                return String.format("%s(%s)", builder.substring(0, index), builder.substring(index));
            }
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #168. Excel Sheet Column Title

[leetcode.com/problems/excel-sheet-column-title/](https://leetcode.com/problems/excel-sheet-column-title/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Convert an integer to its Excel column title (1→A, 26→Z, 27→AA, …).

**Example:** `columnNumber = 1` → `"A"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Use StringBuilder for efficient string building.
2. Process characters left to right; apply transformation rules.
3. Append to builder; handle edge cases (spaces, special chars).
4. Return builder.toString().
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String convertToTitle(int columnNumber) {
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        while(columnNumber > 0) {
            columnNumber--;
            builder.append((char)(columnNumber % 26 + 'A'));
            columnNumber /= 26;
        }
        return builder.reverse().toString();
    }
}
```

- Use char[] instead of String concatenation in loops — avoids O(n²) copies.
- int[26] char frequency array is faster than HashMap for lowercase-only strings.
