#### #348. Design Tic-Tac-Toe

[leetcode.com/problems/design-tictactoe/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-tictactoe/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a Tic-Tac-Toe game that detects a winner in O(1) per move.

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class TicTacToe {
    private int n;
    private int[][] board;
    public TicTacToe(int n) {
        this.n = n;
        this.board = new int[n][n];
    }
    public int move(int row, int col, int player) {
        board[row][col] = player;
        int count = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if(board[row][i] == player) {
                count++;
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
        if(count == n) {
            return player;
        }
        count = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if(board[i][col] == player) {
                count++;
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
        if(count == n) {
            return player;
        }
        count = 0;
        if(row == col) {
            for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if(board[i][i] == player) {
                    count++;
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        if(count == n) {
            return player;
        }
        count = 0;
        if(row + col == n - 1) {
            for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if(board[i][n-1-i] == player) {
                    count++;
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        if(count == n) {
            return player;
        }
        return 0;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #380. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)

[leetcode.com/problems/insert-delete-getrandom-o1/](https://leetcode.com/problems/insert-delete-getrandom-o1/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a set with O(1) insert, remove, and random element retrieval.

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class RandomizedSet {
    List<Integer> list;
    Map<Integer, Integer> map;
    Random random;
    public RandomizedSet() {
        list = new ArrayList<>();
        map = new HashMap<>();
        random = new Random();
    }
    public boolean insert(int val) {
        if(map.containsKey(val)) {
            return false;
        }
        map.put(val, list.size());
        list.add(val);
        return true;
    }
    public boolean remove(int val) {
        if(!map.containsKey(val)) {
            return false;
        }
        int index = map.get(val);
        int lastElement = list.get(list.size() - 1);
        list.set(index, lastElement);
        map.put(lastElement, index);
        list.remove(list.size() - 1);
        map.remove(val);
        return true;
    }
    public int getRandom() {
        return list.get(random.nextInt(list.size()));
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #381. Insert Delete GetRandom O(1) - Duplicates allowed

[leetcode.com/problems/insert-delete-getrandom-o1-duplicates-allowed/](https://leetcode.com/problems/insert-delete-getrandom-o1-duplicates-allowed/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Same as 380 but allows duplicates.

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class RandomizedCollection {
    Map<Integer, Set<Integer>> index;
    List<Integer> nums;
    /** Initialize your data structure here. */
    public RandomizedCollection() {
        index = new HashMap<>();
        nums = new ArrayList<>();
    }
    /** Inserts a value to the collection. Returns true if the collection did not already contain the specified element. */
    public boolean insert(int val) {
        nums.add(val);
        Set<Integer> set = index.getOrDefault(val, new HashSet<>());
        set.add(nums.size() - 1);
        index.put(val, set);
        return set.size() == 1;
    }
    /** Removes a value from the collection. Returns true if the collection contained the specified element. */
    public boolean remove(int val) {
        if (!index.containsKey(val)) {
            return false;
        }
        Iterator<Integer> it = index.get(val).iterator();
        int i = it.next();
        int lastNum = nums.get(nums.size() - 1);
        nums.set(i, lastNum);
        index.get(val).remove(i);
        index.get(lastNum).remove(nums.size() - 1);
        if (i < nums.size() - 1) {
            index.get(lastNum).add(i);
        }
        if (index.get(val).size() == 0) {
            index.remove(val);
        }
        nums.remove(nums.size() - 1);
        return true;
    }
    /** Get a random element from the collection. */
    public int getRandom() {
        return nums.get((int) (Math.random() * nums.size()));
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #519. Random Flip Matrix

[leetcode.com/problems/random-flip-matrix/](https://leetcode.com/problems/random-flip-matrix/) · `O(mn) / O(mn)`

**Q:** Design a matrix with random flip and reset; each 0-cell can be flipped to 1 exactly once.

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(mn) / O(mn)`

```java
class Solution {
    Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
    int m, n, total;
    Random rand = new Random();
    public Solution(int m, int n) {
        this.m = m;
        this.n = n;
        this.total = m * n;
    }
    public int[] flip() {
        // map[0⋯k−1]  0
        // map[k⋯m×n−1]  1
        //  m * n - total  1 total  0
        int x = rand.nextInt(total);
        total--;
        //  x
        int index = map.getOrDefault(x, x);
        //  x  total
        // swap
        // map[x]  map[total]
        map.put(x, map.getOrDefault(total, total));
        return new int[]{index / n, index % n};
    }
    public void reset() {
        total = m * n;
        map.clear();
    }
}
LCP 07.
class Solution {
    int ways, n, k;
    List<List<Integer>> edges;
    public int numWays(int n, int[][] relation, int k) {
        ways = 0;
        this.n = n;
        this.k = k;
        edges = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            edges.add(new ArrayList<>());
        }
        for (int[] edge : relation) {
            int src = edge[0], dst = edge[1];
            edges.get(src).add(dst);
        }
        dfs(0, 0);
        return ways;
    }
    public void dfs(int index, int steps) {
        if (steps == k) {
            if (index == n - 1) {
                ways++;
            }
            return;
        }
        List<Integer> list = edges.get(index);
        for (int nextIndex : list) {
            dfs(nextIndex, steps + 1);
        }
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int numWays(int n, int[][] relation, int k) {
        List<List<Integer>> edges = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            edges.add(new ArrayList<>());
        }
        for (int[] edge : relation) {
            int src = edge[0], dst = edge[1];
            edges.get(src).add(dst);
        }
        int steps = 0;
        Queue<Integer> queue = new LinkedList<>();
        queue.offer(0);
        while (!queue.isEmpty() && steps < k) {
            steps++;
            int size = queue.size();
            for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
                int index = queue.poll();
                List<Integer> list = edges.get(index);
                for (int nextIndex : list) {
                    queue.offer(nextIndex);
                }
            }
        }
        int ways = 0;
        if (steps == k) {
            while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
                if (queue.poll() == n - 1) {
                    ways++;
                }
            }
        }
        return ways;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int numWays(int n, int[][] relation, int k) {
        int[][] dp = new int[k + 1][n];
        dp[0][0] = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) {
            for (int[] edge : relation) {
                int src = edge[0], dst = edge[1];
                //  dp[i+1][dst] dst
                //  k  update  bellman ford
                dp[i + 1][dst] += dp[i][src];
            }
        }
        return dp[k][n - 1];
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #622. Design Circular Queue

[leetcode.com/problems/design-circular-queue/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-circular-queue/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a circular queue (ring buffer) with fixed capacity.

**Example:** `MyCircularQueue(3), enQueue(1), enQueue(2), Front()` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyCircularQueue {
    int[] queue;
    int head;
    int tail;
    int size;
    public MyCircularQueue(int k) {
        queue = new int[k];
        head = -1;
        tail = 0;
        size = 0;
    }
    public boolean enQueue(int value) {
        if(size >= queue.length) {
            return false;
        }
        head = (head + 1) % queue.length;
        queue[head] = value;
        size++;
        return true;
    }
    public boolean deQueue() {
        if(size == 0) {
            return false;
        }
        int val = queue[tail];
        tail = (tail + 1) % queue.length;
        size--;
        return true;
    }
    public int Front() {
        if(size == 0) {
            return -1;
        }
        return queue[tail];
    }
    public int Rear() {
        // System.out.println(head  + " " + tail);
        if(size == 0) {
            return -1;
        }
        return queue[head];
    }
    public boolean isEmpty() {
        return size == 0;
    }
    public boolean isFull() {
        return size == queue.length;
    }
}
class MyCircularQueue {
    int[] queue;
    int front;
    int rear;
    int capacity;
    public MyCircularQueue(int k) {
        queue = new int[k+1];
        front = 0;
        rear = 0;
        capacity = k + 1;
    }
    public boolean enQueue(int value) {
        if(isFull()) {
            return false;
        }
        queue[rear] = value;
        rear = (rear + 1) % capacity;
        return true;
    }
    public boolean deQueue() {
        if(isEmpty()) {
            return false;
        }
        front = (front + 1) % capacity;
        return true;
    }
    public int Front() {
        return isEmpty() ? -1 : queue[front];
    }
    public int Rear() {
        int tail = (rear - 1 + capacity) % capacity;
        return isEmpty() ? -1 : queue[tail];
    }
    public boolean isEmpty() {
        return front == rear;
    }
    public boolean isFull() {
        return (rear + 1) % capacity == front;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #641. Design Circular Deque

[leetcode.com/problems/design-circular-deque/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-circular-deque/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a double-ended circular queue with maxSize capacity.

**Example:** `MyCircularDeque(3), insertFront(1), insertLast(2), getFront()` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyCircularDeque {
    // frontrearrear1
    private int capacity;
    private int[] deque;
    private int front;
    private int rear;
    /** Initialize your data structure here. Set the size of the deque to be k. */
    public MyCircularDeque(int k) {
        capacity = k + 1;
        deque = new int[capacity];
        front = 0;
        rear = 0;
    }
    /** Adds an item at the front of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful. */
    public boolean insertFront(int value) {
        if(isFull()){
            return false;
        }
        front = (front - 1 + capacity) % capacity;
        deque[front] = value;
        return true;
    }
    /** Adds an item at the rear of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful. */
    public boolean insertLast(int value) {
        if(isFull()){
            return false;
        }
        deque[rear] = value;
        rear = (rear + 1) % capacity;
        return true;
    }
    /** Deletes an item from the front of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful. */
    public boolean deleteFront() {
        if(isEmpty()){
            return false;
        }
        front = (front + 1) % capacity;
        return true;
    }
    /** Deletes an item from the rear of Deque. Return true if the operation is successful. */
    public boolean deleteLast() {
        if(isEmpty()){
            return false;
        }
        rear = (rear - 1 + capacity) % capacity;
        return true;
    }
    /** Get the front item from the deque. */
    public int getFront() {
        if(isEmpty()){
            return - 1;
        }
        return deque[front];
    }
    /** Get the last item from the deque. */
    public int getRear() {
        if(isEmpty()){
            return - 1;
        }
        return deque[(rear - 1 + capacity) % capacity];
    }
    /** Checks whether the circular deque is empty or not. */
    public boolean isEmpty() {
        return front == rear;
    }
    /** Checks whether the circular deque is full or not. */
    public boolean isFull() {
        return (rear + 1) % capacity == front;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #705. Design HashSet

[leetcode.com/problems/design-hashset/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-hashset/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a HashSet with add, remove, and contains operations.

**Example:** `MyHashSet.add(1), add(2), contains(1)` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyHashSet {
    private static final int BASE = 769;
    private LinkedList[] data;
    public MyHashSet() {
        data = new LinkedList[BASE];
        for (int i = 0; i < BASE; ++i) {
            data[i] = new LinkedList<>();
        }
    }
    //
    //  hh  hh  h+1,h+2,h+3
    //
    public void add(int key) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Integer> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Integer element = iterator.next();
            if (element == key) {
                return;
            }
        }
        data[h].offerLast(key);
    }
    public void remove(int key) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Integer> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Integer element = iterator.next();
            if (element == key) {
                data[h].remove(element);
                return;
            }
        }
    }
    /** Returns true if this set contains the specified element */
    public boolean contains(int key) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Integer> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Integer element = iterator.next();
            if (element == key) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
    private static int hash(int key) {
        return key % BASE;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #706. Design HashMap

[leetcode.com/problems/design-hashmap/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-hashmap/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a HashMap with put, get, and remove operations.

**Example:** `MyHashMap.put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1)` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyHashMap {
    private class Pair {
        private int key;
        private int value;
        public Pair(int key, int value) {
            this.key = key;
            this.value = value;
        }
        public int getKey() {
            return key;
        }
        public int getValue() {
            return value;
        }
        public void setValue(int value) {
            this.value = value;
        }
    }
    private static final int BASE = 769;
    private LinkedList[] data;
    /** Initialize your data structure here. */
    public MyHashMap() {
        data = new LinkedList[BASE];
        for (int i = 0; i < BASE; ++i) {
            data[i] = new LinkedList<>();
        }
    }
    /** value will always be non-negative. */
    public void put(int key, int value) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Pair> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Pair pair = iterator.next();
            if (pair.getKey() == key) {
                pair.setValue(value);
                return;
            }
        }
        data[h].offerLast(new Pair(key, value));
    }
    /** Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or -1 if this map contains no mapping for the key */
    public int get(int key) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Pair> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Pair pair = iterator.next();
            if (pair.getKey() == key) {
                return pair.value;
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
    /** Removes the mapping of the specified value key if this map contains a mapping for the key */
    public void remove(int key) {
        int h = hash(key);
        Iterator<Pair> iterator = data[h].iterator();
        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
            Pair pair = iterator.next();
            if (pair.key == key) {
                data[h].remove(pair);
                return;
            }
        }
    }
    private static int hash(int key) {
        return key % BASE;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #707. Design Linked List

[leetcode.com/problems/design-linked-list/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-linked-list/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Design a doubly linked list with index-based get, addAtHead, addAtTail, addAtIndex, deleteAtIndex.

**Example:** `MyLinkedList.addAtHead(1), addAtTail(3), addAtIndex(1,2), get(1)` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyLinkedList {
    class ListNode {
        public int val = 0;
        public ListNode next = null;
        public ListNode() {}
        public ListNode(int x) {
            val = x;
        }
    }
    ListNode dummy;
    ListNode tail;
    int length;
    public MyLinkedList() {
        dummy = new ListNode(-1);
        tail = dummy;
        length = 0;
    }
    public ListNode getPrevious(int index) {
        ListNode current = dummy;
        for(int i = 0; i < index; i++) {
            current = current.next;
        }
        return current;
    }
    public int get(int index) {
        if (index < 0 || index >= length) {
            return -1;
        }
        return getPrevious(index).next.val;
    }
    public void addAtHead(int val) {
        ListNode node = new ListNode(val);
        node.next = dummy.next;
        dummy.next = node;
        if(tail == dummy) {
            tail = node;
        }
        length++;
    }
    public void addAtTail(int val) {
        ListNode node = new ListNode(val);
        tail.next = node;
        tail = tail.next;
        length++;
    }
    public void addAtIndex(int index, int val) {
        if (index > length) {
            return;
        } else if (index == length) {
            addAtTail(val);
            return;
        } else if (index <= 0) {
            addAtHead(val);
            return;
        }
        ListNode previous = getPrevious(index);
        ListNode node = new ListNode(val);
        node.next = previous.next;
        previous.next = node;
        length++;
    }
    public void deleteAtIndex(int index) {
        if (index < 0 || index >= length) {
            return;
        }
        ListNode previous = getPrevious(index);
        if(tail == previous.next) {
            tail = previous;
        }
        previous.next = previous.next.next;
        length--;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #729. 我的日程安排表 I

[leetcode.com/problems/i/](https://leetcode.com/problems/i/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Book time intervals on a calendar; return false if overlap exists.

**Example:** `MyCalendar.book(10,20), book(15,25), book(20,30)` → `[true,false,true]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class MyCalendar {
    TreeMap<Integer, Integer> treemap;
    public MyCalendar() {
        this.treemap = new TreeMap<>();
    }
    //  (floorkey, flookey_value)  (start, end) (ceilingkey, ceilingkey_value)
    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
        Integer floorkey = treemap.floorKey(start);
        if(floorkey != null && treemap.get(floorkey) > start) {
            return false;
        }
        Integer ceilingkey = treemap.ceilingKey(start);
        if(ceilingkey != null && end > ceilingkey) {
            return false;
        }
        treemap.put(start, end);
        return true;
    }
}
class MyCalendar {
    private class Node {
        public int index;
        public int flag;
        public Node(int index, int flag) {
            this.index = index;
            this.flag = flag;
        }
    }
    private List<Node> list;
    public MyCalendar() {
        list = new ArrayList<>();
    }
    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
        Node startNode = new Node(start, 1);
        Node endNode = new Node(end, -1);
        list.add(startNode);
        list.add(endNode);
        Collections.sort(list, (a, b) -> (a.index != b.index ? a.index - b.index : a.flag - b.flag));
        int count = 0;
        int maxcount = 0;
        for(Node node: list) {
            if(node.flag == 1) {
                count++;
            } else {
                count--;
            }
            maxcount = Math.max(maxcount, count);
        }
        if(maxcount == 2) {
            list.remove(startNode);
            list.remove(endNode);
        }
        return maxcount <= 1 ? true : false;
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #731. 我的日程安排表 II

[leetcode.com/problems/ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/ii/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Book events; return number of existing bookings overlapping the new booking.

**Example:** `book(10,20), book(50,60), book(10,40), book(5,15)` → `[true,true,true,false]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class MyCalendarTwo {
    private class Node {
        public int index;
        public int flag;
        public Node(int index, int flag) {
            this.index = index;
            this.flag = flag;
        }
    }
    private List<Node> list;
    public MyCalendarTwo() {
        list = new ArrayList<>();
    }
    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
        Node startNode = new Node(start, 1);
        Node endNode = new Node(end, -1);
        list.add(startNode);
        list.add(endNode);
        Collections.sort(list, (a, b) -> (a.index != b.index ? a.index - b.index : a.flag - b.flag));
        int count = 0;
        int maxcount = 0;
        for(Node node: list) {
            if(node.flag == 1) {
                count++;
            } else {
                count--;
            }
            maxcount = Math.max(maxcount, count);
        }
        if(maxcount >= 3) {
            list.remove(startNode);
            list.remove(endNode);
        }
        return maxcount <= 2 ? true : false;
    }
}
class MyCalendarTwo {
    Map<Integer, Integer> map;
    public MyCalendarTwo() {
        this.map = new TreeMap<>();
    }
    // treemap<Integer, Integer> ( key xvalue: )
    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
        map.put(start, map.getOrDefault(start, 0) + 1);
        map.put(end, map.getOrDefault(end, 0) - 1);
        int timesBooked = 0;
        for(int val : map.values()) {
            timesBooked += val;
            if(timesBooked > 2) {
                map.put(start, map.get(start) - 1);
                map.put(end, map.get(end) + 1);
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #732. My Calendar III

[leetcode.com/problems/my-calendar-iii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/my-calendar-iii/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Book events; return the maximum k-booking (k events overlap at same time).

**Example:** `book(10,20), book(50,60), book(10,40), book(5,15)` → `[true,true,true,false]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class MyCalendarThree {
    TreeMap<Integer, Integer> delta;
    public MyCalendarThree() {
        delta = new TreeMap();
    }
    public int book(int start, int end) {
        delta.put(start, delta.getOrDefault(start, 0) + 1);
        delta.put(end, delta.getOrDefault(end, 0) - 1);
        int active = 0, result = 0;
        for (int d: delta.values()) {
            active += d;
            result = Math.max(result, active);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #933. Number of Recent Calls

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-recent-calls/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-recent-calls/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Find the number of requests made in the last 3000 milliseconds.

**Example:** `hit(1), hit(1), hit(2), getHits(4)` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class RecentCounter {
    //  3000  ping  ping  t  ping  t - 3000  ping
    Deque<Integer> queue;
    public RecentCounter() {
        queue = new ArrayDeque();
    }
    public int ping(int t) {
        queue.addLast(t);
        while (queue.getFirst() < t - 3000)
        queue.removeFirst();
        return queue.size();
    }
}
```

- Use sentinel head/tail nodes in doubly linked list to eliminate null checks.
- Lazy deletion: mark nodes deleted in HashMap instead of actually removing from list.

---

#### #1570. Dot Product of Two Sparse Vectors

[leetcode.com/problems/dot-product-of-two-sparse-vectors/](https://leetcode.com/problems/dot-product-of-two-sparse-vectors/) · `O(1) per op / O(n)`

**Q:** Compute the dot product of two sparse vectors efficiently.

**Algorithm:**
1. Choose data structures based on required operation complexities.
2. HashMap provides O(1) lookup; doubly linked list provides O(1) insert/delete.
3. On get: retrieve from map, update ordering structure.
4. On put: evict if at capacity, insert new entry, update map.
`O(1) per op / O(n)`

```java
class SparseVector {
    Map<Integer, Integer> nums1;
    SparseVector(int[] nums) {
        nums1 = new HashMap<>();
        for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if(nums[i] != 0) {
                nums1.put(i, nums[i]);
            }
        }
    }
    // Return the dotProduct of two sparse vectors
    public int dotProduct(SparseVector vec) {
        int result = 0;
        Map<Integer, Integer> nums2 = vec.nums1;
        for(int index : nums1.keySet()) {
            if(nums2.containsKey(index)) {
                result += nums1.get(index) * nums2.get(index);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class SparseVector {
    List<int[]> list;
    SparseVector(int[] nums) {
        this.list = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if (nums[i] != 0) {
                this.list.add(new int[]{i, nums[i]});
            }
        }
    }
    // Return the dotProduct of two sparse vectors
    public int dotProduct(SparseVector vec) {
        int index1 = 0, index2 = 0;
        int product = 0;
        while (index1 < this.list.size() && index2 < vec.list.size()) {
            if (this.list.get(index1)[0] == vec.list.get(index2)[0]) {
                product += this.list.get(index1)[1] * vec.list.get(index2)[1];
                index1++;
                index2++;
            } else if (this.list.get(index1)[0] < vec.list.get(index2)[0]) {
                index1++;
            } else {
                index2++;
            }
        }
        return product;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

### Trie (8 problems)

#### #212. Word Search II

[leetcode.com/problems/word-search-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/word-search-ii/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Find all words from a dictionary that exist in an m×n character board (adjacent cells, no reuse).

**Example:** `board = [["o","a","a","n"],["e","t","a","e"],["i","h","k","r"],["i","f","l","v"]], words = ["oath","pea","eat","rain"]` → `["eat","oath"]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class Solution {
    private int[][] DIRS = {{-1, 0}, {1, 0}, {0, 1}, {0, -1}};
    public List<String> findWords(char[][] board, String[] words) {
        Set<String> result = new HashSet<>();
        int rows = board.length, columns = board[0].length;
        boolean[][] used = new boolean[rows][columns];
        Trie trie = new Trie();
        for(String word : words) {
            trie.insert(word);
        }
        for(int i = 0; i < rows; i++) {
            for(int j = 0; j < columns; j++) {
                dfs(board, trie, used, i, j, result);
            }
        }
        return new ArrayList<>(result);
    }
    public void dfs(char[][] board, Trie trie, boolean[][] used, int i, int j, Set<String> result) {
        if(!trie.children.containsKey(board[i][j])) {
            return ;
        }
        trie = trie.children.get(board[i][j]);
        if(trie.isWord) {
            result.add(trie.word);
        }
        used[i][j] = true;
        for(int[] neighbor : getNeighbors(board, i, j)) {
            int x = neighbor[0], y = neighbor[1];
            if(!used[x][y]) {
                dfs(board, trie, used, x, y, result);
            }
        }
        used[i][j] = false;
    }
    public boolean inBound(char[][] board, int x, int y) {
        return x >= 0 && x < board.length && y >= 0 && y < board[0].length;
    }
    public List<int[]> getNeighbors(char[][] board, int i, int j) {
        List<int[]> neighbors = new ArrayList<>();
        for(int[] dir : DIRS) {
            int x = i + dir[0], y = j + dir[1];
            if(inBound(board, x, y)) {
                neighbors.add(new int[]{x, y});
            }
        }
        return neighbors;
    }
    public class Trie {
        Map<Character, Trie> children;
        boolean isWord;
        String word;
        public Trie() {
            children = new HashMap<>();
            isWord = false;
            word = null;
        }
        public void insert(String s) {
            Trie current = this;
            for(char ch : s.toCharArray()) {
                current = current.children.computeIfAbsent(ch, trie -> new Trie());
            }
            current.isWord = true;
            current.word = s;
        }
        public Trie searchPrefix(String prefix) {
            Trie current = this;
            for(char ch : prefix.toCharArray()) {
                if(current.children.containsKey(ch)) {
                    current = current.children.get(ch);
                } else {
                    return null;
                }
            }
            return current;
        }
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).
- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #208. Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)

[leetcode.com/problems/implement-trie-prefix-tree/](https://leetcode.com/problems/implement-trie-prefix-tree/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Implement a Trie with insert, search, and startsWith operations.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class Trie {
    Map<Character, Trie> children;
    boolean isEnd;
    public Trie() {
        children = new HashMap<>();
        isEnd = false;
    }
    public void insert(String word) {
        Trie current = this;
        for(char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
            current = current.children.computeIfAbsent(ch, trie -> new Trie());
            // if(!current.children.containsKey(ch)) {
                //     current.children.put(ch, new Trie());
                // }
            current = current.children.get(ch);
        }
        current.isEnd = true;
    }
    public boolean search(String word) {
        Trie node = searchPrefix(word);
        return node != null && node.isEnd;
    }
    public boolean startsWith(String prefix) {
        return searchPrefix(prefix) != null;
    }
    public Trie searchPrefix(String word) {
        Trie current = this;
        for(char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
            if(!current.children.containsKey(ch)) {
                return null;
            }
            current = current.children.get(ch);
        }
        return current;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #211. Design Add and Search Words Data Structure

[leetcode.com/problems/design-add-and-search-words-data-structure/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-add-and-search-words-data-structure/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Design a data structure supporting addWord and search (with '.' wildcard) for words.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class WordDictionary {
    Map<Character, WordDictionary> children;
    boolean isWord;
    public WordDictionary() {
        children = new HashMap<>();
        isWord = false;
    }
    public void addWord(String word) {
        WordDictionary current = this;
        for(char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
            current = current.children.computeIfAbsent(ch, wordDictionary -> new WordDictionary());
        }
        current.isWord = true;
    }
    public boolean search(String word) {
        return search(word.toCharArray(), 0, this);
    }
    public boolean search(char[] arr, int i, WordDictionary wordDictionary) {
        if(i == arr.length) {
            return wordDictionary.isWord;
        }
        boolean match = false;
        if(arr[i] == '.') {
            for(Map.Entry<Character, WordDictionary> entry : wordDictionary.children.entrySet()) {
                match |= search(arr, i+1, entry.getValue());
            }
        } else {
            if(wordDictionary.children.containsKey(arr[i])) {
                return search(arr, i+1, wordDictionary.children.get(arr[i]));
            } else {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return match;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #336. Palindrome Pairs

[leetcode.com/problems/palindrome-pairs/](https://leetcode.com/problems/palindrome-pairs/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Find all palindrome pairs among a list of unique words.

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class Solution {
    public List<List<Integer>> palindromePairs(String[] words) {
        Trie trie1 = new Trie();
        Trie trie2 = new Trie();
        int n = words.length;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            trie1.insert(words[i], i);
            StringBuffer temp = new StringBuffer(words[i]);
            temp.reverse();
            trie2.insert(temp.toString(), i);
        }
        List<List<Integer>> ret = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            int[][] rec = manacher(words[i]);
            int[] id1 = trie2.query(words[i]);
            words[i] = new StringBuffer(words[i]).reverse().toString();
            int[] id2 = trie1.query(words[i]);
            int m = words[i].length();
            int allId = id1[m];
            if (allId != -1 && allId != i) {
                ret.add(Arrays.asList(i, allId));
            }
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                if (rec[j][0] != 0) {
                    int leftId = id2[m - j - 1];
                    if (leftId != -1 && leftId != i) {
                        ret.add(Arrays.asList(leftId, i));
                    }
                }
                if (rec[j][1] != 0) {
                    int rightId = id1[j];
                    if (rightId != -1 && rightId != i) {
                        ret.add(Arrays.asList(i, rightId));
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return ret;
    }
    public int[][] manacher(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        StringBuffer temp = new StringBuffer("#");
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (i > 0) {
                temp.append('*');
            }
            temp.append(s.charAt(i));
        }
        temp.append('!');
        int m = n * 2;
        int[] len = new int[m];
        int[][] ret = new int[n][2];
        int p = 0, maxn = -1;
        for (int i = 1; i < m; i++) {
            len[i] = maxn >= i ? Math.min(len[2 * p - i], maxn - i) : 0;
            while (temp.charAt(i - len[i] - 1) == temp.charAt(i + len[i] + 1)) {
                len[i]++;
            }
            if (i + len[i] > maxn) {
                p = i;
                maxn = i + len[i];
            }
            if (i - len[i] == 1) {
                ret[(i + len[i]) / 2][0] = 1;
            }
            if (i + len[i] == m - 1) {
                ret[(i - len[i]) / 2][1] = 1;
            }
        }
        return ret;
    }
}
class Trie {
    class Node {
        int[] ch = new int[26];
        int flag;
        public Node() {
            flag = -1;
        }
    }
    List<Node> tree = new ArrayList<>();
    public Trie() {
        tree.add(new Node());
    }
    public void insert(String s, int id) {
        int len = s.length(), add = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            int x = s.charAt(i) - 'a';
            if (tree.get(add).ch[x] == 0) {
                tree.add(new Node());
                tree.get(add).ch[x] = tree.size() - 1;
            }
            add = tree.get(add).ch[x];
        }
        tree.get(add).flag = id;
    }
    public int[] query(String s) {
        int len = s.length(), add = 0;
        int[] ret = new int[len + 1];
        Arrays.fill(ret, -1);
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            ret[i] = tree.get(add).flag;
            int x = s.charAt(i) - 'a';
            if (tree.get(add).ch[x] == 0) {
                return ret;
            }
            add = tree.get(add).ch[x];
        }
        ret[len] = tree.get(add).flag;
        return ret;
    }
}
```

- Use 1D rolling array if dp[i] only depends on previous row — reduces space from O(mn) to O(n).

---

#### #648. Replace Words

[leetcode.com/problems/replace-words/](https://leetcode.com/problems/replace-words/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Given a sentence and a list of roots, replace each word with its root if one exists.

**Example:** `sentence = "i like leetcoding", searchWord = "leet"` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class Solution {
    //
    public String replaceWords(List<String> roots, String sentence) {
        TrieNode trie = new TrieNode();
        for (String root: roots) {
            TrieNode current = trie;
            for (char letter: root.toCharArray()) {
                if (current.children[letter - 'a'] == null)
                current.children[letter - 'a'] = new TrieNode();
                current = current.children[letter - 'a'];
            }
            current.word = root;
        }
        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        for (String word: sentence.split("\\s+")) {
            if (result.length() > 0)
            result.append(" ");
            TrieNode current = trie;
            for (char letter: word.toCharArray()) {
                if (current.children[letter - 'a'] == null || current.word != null)
                break;
                current = current.children[letter - 'a'];
            }
            result.append(current.word != null ? current.word : word);
        }
        return result.toString();
    }
}
class TrieNode {
    TrieNode[] children;
    String word;
    TrieNode() {
        children = new TrieNode[26];
    }
}
```

- Store word index or count at isEnd instead of boolean for multi-word queries.
- Compress single-child chains into Patricia trie to reduce space.

#### #676. Implement Magic Dictionary

[leetcode.com/problems/implement-magic-dictionary/](https://leetcode.com/problems/implement-magic-dictionary/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Design a data structure with addWord and search supporting '.' wildcard.

**Example:** `MagicDictionary.buildDict(["hello","leetcode"]), search("hello")` → `false`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class MagicDictionary {
    Trie trie = new Trie();
    public MagicDictionary() {
    }
    public void buildDict(String[] dictionary) {
        for(String word : dictionary){
            trie.insert(word);
        }
    }
    public boolean search(String searchWord) {
        return trie.find(searchWord,0,trie.root,1);
    }
    class Trie{
        Node root = new Node();
        //start: cur: cnt:
        boolean find(String s,int start,Node current,int count){
            if(current == null)return false;
            if(start == s.length()) {
                return current.isWord && count == 0;
            }
            int index = s.charAt(start) - 'a';
            if(current.child[index] != null && find(s,start+1,current.child[index],count)) {
                return true;
            }
            if(count > 0){
                for(int i = 0; i < 26;i++){
                    if(i==index || current.child[i] == null)continue;
                    if(find(s,start+1,current.child[i],count-1))return true;
                }
            }
            return false;
        }
        void insert(String s){
            Node current = root;
            for(char ch:s.toCharArray()){
                int index = ch-'a';
                if(current.child[index]==null)current.child[index] = new Node();
                current=current.child[index];
            }
            current.isWord=true;
        }
    }
    class Node{
        Node[] child = new Node[26];
        boolean isWord;
    }
}
```

- Store word index or count at isEnd instead of boolean for multi-word queries.
- Compress single-child chains into Patricia trie to reduce space.

---

#### #720. Longest Word in Dictionary

[leetcode.com/problems/longest-word-in-dictionary/](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-word-in-dictionary/) · `O(nL) / O(nL)`

**Q:** Find the longest word in a dictionary that can be built one character at a time.

**Example:** `words = ["w","wo","wor","worl","world"]` → `"world"`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(nL) / O(nL)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String longestWord(String[] words) {
        Trie trie = new Trie();
        int index = 0;
        for (String word: words) {
            trie.insert(word, ++index); //indexed by 1
        }
        trie.words = words;
        return trie.dfs();
    }
}
class Node {
    char c;
    HashMap<Character, Node> children = new HashMap();
    int end;
    public Node(char c){
        this.c = c;
    }
}
class Trie {
    Node root;
    String[] words;
    public Trie() {
        root = new Node('0');
    }
    public void insert(String word, int index) {
        Node current = root;
        for (char c: word.toCharArray()) {
            current.children.putIfAbsent(c, new Node(c));
            current = current.children.get(c);
        }
        current.end = index;
    }
    public String dfs() {
        String result = "";
        Stack<Node> stack = new Stack();
        stack.push(root);
        while (!stack.empty()) {
            Node node = stack.pop();
            if (node.end > 0 || node == root) {
                if (node != root) {
                    String word = words[node.end - 1];
                    if (word.length() > result.length() ||
                    word.length() == result.length() && word.compareTo(result) < 0) {
                        result = word;
                    }
                }
                for (Node nei: node.children.values()) {
                    stack.push(nei);
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Store word index or count at isEnd instead of boolean for multi-word queries.
- Compress single-child chains into Patricia trie to reduce space.

---

#### #1233. Remove Sub-Folders from the Filesystem

[leetcode.com/problems/remove-subfolders-from-the-filesystem/](https://leetcode.com/problems/remove-subfolders-from-the-filesystem/) · `O(n log n) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Build Trie: TrieNode with children[26] and isEnd flag.
2. Insert words: walk chars, create nodes, mark isEnd at last char.
3. Search: walk chars; return false if child missing, return isEnd at end.
4. For prefix queries: walk prefix chars, return true if all exist.
`O(n log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    private static final String SPLIT = "/";
    List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
    public List<String> removeSubfolders(String[] folder) {
        Arrays.sort(folder);
        log("");
        Trie trie = new Trie();
        for (String path : folder) {
            trie.insert(path);
        }
        countTrie(trie);
        return result;
    }
    private void countTrie(Trie trie) {
        if (trie.child.isEmpty()) {
            return;
        }
        for (String key : trie.child.keySet()) {
            result.add(key);
            countTrie(trie.child.get(key));
        }
    }
    class Trie {
        TreeMap<String, Trie> child = new TreeMap<>();
        /** Initialize your data structure here. */
        public Trie() {
        }
        /** Inserts a word into the trie. */
        public void insert(String path) {
            Trie root = this;
            Set<String> set = root.child.keySet();
            boolean insert = true;
            for (String rootKey : set) {
                if ((path + SPLIT).startsWith(rootKey + SPLIT)) {
                    insert = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (insert) {
                root.child.put(path, new Trie());
            }
        }
        @Override
        public String toString() {
            return "" + child;
        }
    }
    private static <T extends Object> void log(T logObj) {
    }
}
```

- After sorting, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of linear scan for lookups.

### Bit Manipulation (15 problems)

#### #268. Missing Number

[leetcode.com/problems/missing-number/](https://leetcode.com/problems/missing-number/) · `O(n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the single missing number in an array of n distinct integers in range [0, n].

**Example:** `nums = [3,0,1]` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. XOR approach: XOR all indices 0..n and all nums.
2. Paired elements cancel; the missing index remains.
3. Alt: sum = n*(n+1)/2 - sum(nums).
`O(n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int missingNumber(int[] nums) {
        int result = 0;
        int n = nums.length;
        for(int i = 0 ; i <= n; i++) {
            result = result ^ i;
        }
        for(int num : nums) {
            result = result ^ num;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #136. Single Number

[leetcode.com/problems/single-number/](https://leetcode.com/problems/single-number/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the single element in an array where every other element appears exactly twice.

**Example:** `nums = [2,2,1]` → `1`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return -1;
        }
        return threeWayPartition(nums, 0, nums.length);
    }
    private void swap(int[] nums, int i, int j) {
        int temp = nums[i];
        nums[i] = nums[j];
        nums[j] = temp;
    }
    private int threeWayPartition(int[] nums, int start, int end) {
        int left = start, current = start, right = end - 1;
        int pivot = nums[(left + right) / 2];
        while (current <= right) {
            if (nums[current] < pivot) {
                swap(nums, left, current);
                left++;
                current++;
            } else if (nums[current] == pivot) {
                current++;
            } else {
                swap(nums, right, current);
                right--;
            }
        }
        // after the partition
        // left range: [start, left)  len = left - start
        // mid range: [left, current)  len = current - left
        // right range: [current, end) len = end - current
        if(current - left == 1) {
            return nums[left];
        } else if((left - start) % 2 == 1) {
            return threeWayPartition(nums, start, left);
        } else {
            return threeWayPartition(nums, current, end);
        }
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        int result = 0;
        for(int num : nums) {
            result ^= num;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

---

#### #137. Single Number II

[leetcode.com/problems/single-number-ii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/single-number-ii/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the single element in an array where every other element appears exactly three times.

**Example:** `nums = [2,2,3,2]` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> freq = new HashMap<>();
        for (int num : nums) {
            freq.put(num, freq.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : freq.entrySet()) {
            int num = entry.getKey(), occ = entry.getValue();
            if (occ == 1) {
                result = num;
                break;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
            int total = 0;
            for (int num: nums) {
                total += ((num >> i) & 1);
            }
            if (total % 3 != 0) {
                result |= (1 << i);
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

---

#### #260. Single Number III

[leetcode.com/problems/single-number-iii/](https://leetcode.com/problems/single-number-iii/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find two non-repeating numbers in an array where all others appear twice.

**Example:** `nums = [1,2,1,3,2,5]` → `[3,5]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Set left=0, right=n-1 (or on answer space).
2. Compute mid = left + (right-left)/2 to avoid overflow.
3. If nums[mid] satisfies condition: lock bound (shrink toward answer).
4. Else: eliminate that half.
5. Return left (or right) after loop.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
    public int[] singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        if(nums == null || nums.length == 0) {
            return new int[]{};
        }
        threeWayPartition(nums, 0, nums.length);
        int[] result = new int[2];
        result[0] = result.get(0);
        result[1] = result.get(1);
        return result;
    }
    private void swap(int[] nums, int i, int j) {
        int temp = nums[i];
        nums[i] = nums[j];
        nums[j] = temp;
    }
    private void threeWayPartition(int[] nums, int start, int end) {
        if(result.size() == 2 || start >= end) {
            return ;
        }
        int left = start, current = start, right = end - 1;
        int pivot = nums[(left + right) / 2];
        while (current <= right) {
            if (nums[current] < pivot) {
                swap(nums, left, current);
                left++;
                current++;
            } else if (nums[current] == pivot) {
                current++;
            } else {
                swap(nums, right, current);
                right--;
            }
        }
        // after the partition
        // left range: [start, left)  len = left - start
        // mid range: [left, current)  len = current - left
        // right range: [current, end) len = end - current
        if(current - left == 1) {
            System.out.println(nums[left]);
            result.add(nums[left]);
        }
        threeWayPartition(nums, start, left);
        threeWayPartition(nums, current, end);
    }
}
class Solution {
    //  x & -x  x  1 l
    public int[] singleNumber(int[] nums) {
        int xorsum = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            xorsum ^= num;
        }
        //
        int lsb = (xorsum == Integer.MIN_VALUE ? xorsum : xorsum & (-xorsum));
        int type1 = 0, type2 = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            if ((num & lsb) != 0) {
                type1 ^= num;
            } else {
                type2 ^= num;
            }
        }
        return new int[]{type1, type2};
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #190. Reverse Bits

[leetcode.com/problems/reverse-bits/](https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-bits/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Reverse all bits of a 32-bit unsigned integer.

**Example:** `n = 00000010100101000001111010011100` → `964176192`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
public class Solution {
    //  n  32  n  rev
    public int reverseBits(int n) {
        int rev = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32 && n != 0; ++i) {
            rev |= (n & 1) << (31 - i);
            //  n
            n >>>= 1;
        }
        return rev;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #191. Number of 1 Bits

[leetcode.com/problems/number-of-1-bits/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-1-bits/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Count the number of '1' bits in a 32-bit integer (Hamming weight).

**Example:** `n = 00000000000000000000000000001011` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
public class Solution {
    // you need to treat n as an unsigned value
    public int hammingWeight(int n) {
        return n == 0 ? 0 : 1 + hammingWeight(n & (n - 1));
    }
}
public class Solution {
    // you need to treat n as an unsigned value
    public int hammingWeight(int n) {
        int result = 0;
        for(int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
            if((n & (1 << i)) != 0) {
                result++;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #231. Power of Two

[leetcode.com/problems/power-of-two/](https://leetcode.com/problems/power-of-two/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Determine if a number is a power of two.

**Example:** `n = 16` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isPowerOfTwo(int n) {
        while (n != 0 && n % 2 == 0) {
            n /= 2;
        }
        return n == 1;
    }
}
class Solution {
    //  1
    //  n  1  0
    // −n  n  1
    // (n & (n - 1)) 10
    public boolean isPowerOfTwo(int n) {
        return n > 0 && (n & (n - 1)) == 0;
    }
}
class Solution {
    //  n  1
    public boolean isPowerOfTwo(int n) {
        return n > 0 && (n & -n) == n;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #342. Power of Four

[leetcode.com/problems/power-of-four/](https://leetcode.com/problems/power-of-four/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Determine if a number is a power of four.

**Example:** `n = 16` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isPowerOfFour(int n) {
        while (n != 0 && n % 4 == 0) {
            n /= 4;
        }
        return n == 1;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public boolean isPowerOfFour(int n) {
        // n  1 1
        return n > 0 && (n & (n - 1)) == 0 && (n & 0xaaaaaaaa) == 0;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #318. Maximum Product of Word Lengths

[leetcode.com/problems/maximum-product-of-word-lengths/](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-product-of-word-lengths/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the maximum product of lengths of two words with no common letters.

**Example:** `words = ["abcw","baz","foo","bar","xtfn","abcdef"]` → `16`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    //  26  26
    public int maxProduct(String[] words) {
        int length = words.length;
        int[] masks = new int[length];
        for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
            String word = words[i];
            int wordLength = word.length();
            for (int j = 0; j < wordLength; j++) {
                masks[i] |= 1 << (word.charAt(j) - 'a');
            }
        }
        int max = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < length; j++) {
                if ((masks[i] & masks[j]) == 0) {
                    max = Math.max(max, words[i].length() * words[j].length());
                }
            }
        }
        return max;
    }
}
class Solution {
    //  26  26
    //
    //
    public int maxProduct(String[] words) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
        int length = words.length;
        for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
            int mask = 0;
            String word = words[i];
            int wordLength = word.length();
            for (int j = 0; j < wordLength; j++) {
                mask |= 1 << (word.charAt(j) - 'a');
            }
            if (wordLength > map.getOrDefault(mask, 0)) {
                map.put(mask, wordLength);
            }
        }
        int maxProd = 0;
        Set<Integer> maskSet = map.keySet();
        for (int mask1 : maskSet) {
            int wordLength1 = map.get(mask1);
            for (int mask2 : maskSet) {
                if ((mask1 & mask2) == 0) {
                    int wordLength2 = map.get(mask2);
                    maxProd = Math.max(maxProd, wordLength1 * wordLength2);
                }
            }
        }
        return maxProd;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

#### #338. Counting Bits

[leetcode.com/problems/counting-bits/](https://leetcode.com/problems/counting-bits/) · `O(n) / O(n)`

**Q:** Return an array counting the number of 1 bits for each integer from 0 to n.

**Example:** `n = 5` → `[0,1,1,2,1,2]`

**Algorithm:**
1. dp[0]=0.
2. For i from 1 to n: dp[i] = dp[i>>1] + (i&1).
3. i>>1 = i/2 has one fewer bit set if i was odd, or same if even.
`O(n) / O(n)`

```java
class Solution {
    // x: x / 2
    // x  bits[x] = bits[x / 2] + 1;
    // x : bits[x] = bits[x / 2]
    public int[] countBits(int n) {
        int[] bits = new int[n + 1];
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            bits[i] = bits[i >> 1] + (i & 1);
        }
        return bits;
    }
}
class Solution {
    // x = x & (x - 1)
    //  x  0
    public int[] countBits(int n) {
        int[] bits = new int[n + 1];
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            bits[i] = bits[i & (i - 1)] + 1;
        }
        return bits;
    }
}
```

- dp[i>>1]+(i&1): right-shift gives parent value, +LSB counts the extra bit.

#### #371. Sum of Two Integers

[leetcode.com/problems/sum-of-two-integers/](https://leetcode.com/problems/sum-of-two-integers/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Add two integers without using + or - operators.

**Example:** `a = 1, b = 2` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int getSum(int a, int b) {
        return b == 0 ? a : getSum(a ^ b, (a & b) << 1);
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int getSum(int a, int b) {
        while(b != 0) {
            int carry = (a & b) << 1;
            a = a ^ b;
            b = carry;
        }
        return a;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #405. Convert a Number to Hexadecimal

[leetcode.com/problems/convert-a-number-to-hexadecimal/](https://leetcode.com/problems/convert-a-number-to-hexadecimal/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public String toHex(int num) {
        if (num == 0) {
            return "0";
        }
        StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 7; i >= 0; i--) {
            int val = (num >> (4 * i)) & 0xf;
            if (builder.length() > 0 || val > 0) {  // 0
                char digit = val < 10 ? (char) ('0' + val) : (char) ('a' + val - 10);
                builder.append(digit);
            }
        }
        return builder.toString();
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #461. Hamming Distance

[leetcode.com/problems/hamming-distance/](https://leetcode.com/problems/hamming-distance/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Calculate Hamming distance between two integers (positions where bits differ).

**Example:** `x = 1, y = 4` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int hammingDistance(int x, int y) {
        int s = x ^ y, ret = 0;
        while (s != 0) {
            // ret += s & 1;
            // s >>= 1;
            ret += s % 2;
            s /= 2;
        }
        return ret;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int totalHammingDistance(int[] nums) {
        int result = 0, n = nums.length;
        for (int i = 0; i < 30; ++i) {
            int c = 0;
            for (int val : nums) {
                // i
                c += (val >> i) & 1;
            }
            result += c * (n - c);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #476. Number Complement

[leetcode.com/problems/number-complement/](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-complement/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the complement of an integer (flip all bits up to the most significant bit).

**Example:** `n = 5` → `2`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int findComplement(int num) {
        //  num  1  s
        int s = -1;
        for (int i = 31; i >= 0; i--) {
            if (((num >> i) & 1) != 0) {
                s = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < s; i++) {
            if (((num >> i) & 1) == 0) result |= (1 << i);
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public int findComplement(int num) {
        //  num  1 1
        int highbit = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i <= 30; ++i) {
            if (num >= 1 << i) {
                highbit = i;
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
        int mask = highbit == 30 ? 0x7fffffff : (1 << (highbit + 1)) - 1;
        return num ^ mask;
    }
}
```

- Use Integer.bitCount(n) instead of manual count — intrinsic on modern JVMs.
- XOR of full array finds single non-duplicate; XOR of [1..n] ^ array finds missing number.

#### #957. Prison Cells After N Days

[leetcode.com/problems/prison-cells-after-n-days/](https://leetcode.com/problems/prison-cells-after-n-days/) · `O(1) / O(1)`

**Q:** Find the prison cell configuration after n days of simultaneous updates.

**Example:** `cells = [0,1,0,1,1,0,0,1], n = 7` → `[0,0,1,1,0,0,0,0]`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify bit trick: XOR cancels pairs, n&(n-1) clears lowest set bit, n&(-n) isolates it.
2. Apply bit operation to each element or position.
3. Accumulate result using XOR, AND, OR, or shift.
4. Return final bit pattern or count.
`O(1) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int[] prisonAfterNDays(int[] cells, int N) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> seen = new HashMap();
        // state  = integer representing state of prison
        int state = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) {
            if (cells[i] > 0)
            state ^= 1 << i;
        }
        // While days remaining, simulate a day
        while (N > 0) {
            // If this is a cycle, fast forward by
            // seen.get(state) - N, the period of the cycle.
            if (seen.containsKey(state)) {
                N %= seen.get(state) - N;
            }
            seen.put(state, N);
            if (N >= 1) {
                N--;
                state = nextDay(state);
            }
        }
        // Convert the state back to the required answer.
        int[] result = new int[8];
        for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) {
            if (((state >> i) & 1) > 0) {
                result[i] = 1;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
    public int nextDay(int state) {
        int result = 0;
        // We only loop from 1 to 6 because 0 and 7 are impossible,
        // as those cells only have one neighbor.
        for (int i = 1; i <= 6; ++i) {
            if (((state >> (i-1)) & 1) == ((state >> (i+1)) & 1)) {
                result ^= 1 << i;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    // 14
    public int[] prisonAfterNDays(int[] cells, int N) {
        N = (N - 1) % 14 + 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
            cells = findNext(cells);
        }
        return cells;
    }
    public int[] findNext(int[] cells) {
        int[] array = new int[cells.length];
        for (int i = 1; i < cells.length - 1; i++) {
            if (cells[i - 1] == cells[i + 1]) {
                array[i] = 1;
            } else {
                array[i] = 0;
            }
        }
        return array;
    }
}
```

- Return early on first HashMap hit rather than scanning full input.

### Math (58 problems)

#### #9. Palindrome Number

[leetcode.com/problems/palindrome-number/](https://leetcode.com/problems/palindrome-number/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Determine whether an integer reads the same forward and backward. Negative numbers are never palindromes.

**Example:** `x = 121` → `true`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify mathematical pattern: GCD, modular arithmetic, or number theory.
2. Apply relevant formula or recurrence.
3. Handle overflow using long or modular arithmetic.
4. Return computed value.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isPalindrome(int x) {
        if (x < 0 || (x % 10 == 0 && x != 0)) return false;
        int revertedNumber = 0;
        while (x > revertedNumber) {
            revertedNumber = revertedNumber * 10 + x % 10;
            x /= 10;
        }
        return x == revertedNumber || x == revertedNumber / 10;
    }
}
```

- Use long arithmetic and take modulo after each multiplication to prevent overflow.
- Cache GCD computations when called repeatedly on overlapping pairs.

#### #29. Divide Two Integers

[leetcode.com/problems/divide-two-integers/](https://leetcode.com/problems/divide-two-integers/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Divide two integers without using multiplication, division, or mod. Clamp to 32-bit signed range.

**Example:** `dividend = 10, divisor = 3` → `3`

**Algorithm:**
1. Identify mathematical pattern: GCD, modular arithmetic, or number theory.
2. Apply relevant formula or recurrence.
3. Handle overflow using long or modular arithmetic.
4. Return computed value.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public int divide(int dividend, int divisor) {
        //
        if (dividend == Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
            if (divisor == 1) {
                return Integer.MIN_VALUE;
            }
            if (divisor == -1) {
                return Integer.MAX_VALUE;
            }
        }
        //
        if (divisor == Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
            return dividend == Integer.MIN_VALUE ? 1 : 0;
        }
        //  0
        if (dividend == 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        //
        //
        boolean rev = false;
        if (dividend > 0) {
            dividend = -dividend;
            rev = !rev;
        }
        if (divisor > 0) {
            divisor = -divisor;
            rev = !rev;
        }
        List<Integer> candidates = new ArrayList<>();
        candidates.add(divisor);
        int index = 0;
        //
        while (candidates.get(index) >= dividend - candidates.get(index)) {
            candidates.add(candidates.get(index) + candidates.get(index));
            ++index;
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = candidates.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
            if (candidates.get(i) >= dividend) {
                result += 1 << i;
                dividend -= candidates.get(i);
            }
        }
        return rev ? -result : result;
    }
}
```

- Use long arithmetic and take modulo after each multiplication to prevent overflow.
- Cache GCD computations when called repeatedly on overlapping pairs.

#### #50. Pow(x, n)

[leetcode.com/problems/powx-n/](https://leetcode.com/problems/powx-n/) · `O(log n) / O(1)`

**Q:** Implement pow(x, n) — raise x to the power n (n can be negative).

**Example:** `x = 2.00000, n = 10` → `1024.00000`

**Algorithm:**
1. Fast exponentiation: if exp odd → multiply result by base.
2. Square base and halve exponent each iteration.
3. Apply modulo at each step if needed.
`O(log n) / O(1)`

```java
class Solution {
    public double myPow(double x, int n) {
        return n >= 0 ? quickPow(x, n) : 1.0 / quickPow(x, -n);
    }
    public double quickPow(double x, int n) {
        if(n == 0) {
            return 1;
        }
        double pow = quickPow(x, n / 2);
        return n % 2 == 0 ? pow * pow : pow * pow * x;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public double myPow(double x, int n) {
        return n > 0 ? quickPow(x, n) : 1.0 / quickPow(x, -n);
    }
    public double quickPow(double x, int n) {
        double result = 1;
        while(n != 0) {
            if(n % 2 != 0) {
                result *= x;
            }
            x *= x;
            n = n / 2;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public double myPow(double x, int n) {
        return n > 0 ? quickPow(x, n) : 1.0 / quickPow(x, -n);
    }
    public double quickPow(double x, int n) {
        double pow = 1.0;
        while(n > 0) {
            System.out.println(pow);
            if(n % 2 == 1) {
                pow *= x;
            }
            x *= x;
            n /= 2;
        }
        return pow;
    }
}
class Solution {
    public double myPow(double x, int n) {
        return n >= 0 ? pow(x, n) : 1 / pow(x, -n);
    }
    public double pow(double x, int n) {
        double result = 1;
        for(int i = 0; i <= 31; i++) {
            if(((n >> i) & 1) == 1) {
                result = result * x;
            }
            x *= x;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```

- Iterative fast power with bit manipulation avoids recursion stack; same O(log n) complexity.
